<content>1: TASK: Fix headless spawn to verify session creation and surface prompt_async errors instead of silently reporting success.
2: 
3: ORIENTATION_FRAME: Headless codex spawns report success but the agent is silently dead. orch-go fires CreateSession + prompt_async and walks away. If auth/model fails, the error is only logged server-side. User sees 'Spawned agent (headless)' when the agent is DOA.
4: 
5: PROJECT_DIR: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go
6: SESSION SCOPE: Small
7: 
8: ## Root Cause (from orch-go-1051)
9: 1. prompt_async returns 204 immediately. Errors only emitted as session.error SSE events.
10: 2. Session listing is project-scoped by x-opencode-directory header.
11: 3. orch-go never verifies the session after creating it.
12: 
13: ## The Fix
14: In pkg/opencode/client.go: After CreateSession + SendMessageInDirectory, verify session exists with GET /session/:id and correct directory header. Brief SSE subscribe (2-3s) to catch session.error. Return error if session dead.
15: 
16: In pkg/orch/extraction.go (headless spawn path): Check verification result. Print error instead of success if session is dead.
17: 
18: ## Scope
19: IN: Post-spawn verification in orch-go. Surface errors.
20: OUT: No OpenCode server changes.
21: 
22: ## Verification
23: go test ./pkg/opencode/ ./pkg/orch/
24:

51: SPAWN TIER: full
52: 
53: 📚 FULL TIER: This spawn requires SYNTHESIS.md for knowledge externalization.
54:    Document your findings, decisions, and learnings in SYNTHESIS.md before completing.
55: 
56: 
57: 
58: ## PRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from kb context)
59: 
60: **Query:** "headless spawn verify"
61: 
62: ### Constraints (MUST respect)
63: - Agents must not spawn more than 3 iterations without human review
64:   - Reason: Prevents runaway iteration loops like 12 tmux fallback tests in 9 minutes
65: - orch complete must verify SYNTHESIS.md exists and is not placeholder before closing issue
66:   - Reason: 70% of agents completed without synthesis in 24h chaos period
67: - Untracked spawns (--no-track) generate placeholder beads IDs that fail bd comment commands
68:   - Reason: Beads IDs like orch-go-untracked-* don't exist in database, causing bd comment to fail with 'issue not found' - this is expected behavior, not a bug
69: - Worker spawns must set ORCH_WORKER=1 to skip orchestrator skill loading
70:   - Reason: Orchestrator skill (1,251 lines ~37k tokens) is auto-loaded by session-context plugin for all orch projects but is unnecessary for worker sessions, wastes context budget
71: - Worker agents must test spawn functionality via unit tests and code review, not end-to-end spawning
72:   - Reason: Prevents recursive spawn testing incidents while still enabling verification
73: - kb context command hangs on some queries
74:   - Reason: Blocks orch spawn from returning, use --skip-artifact-check as workaround
75: - Orchestrator is AI, not Dylan - Dylan interacts with AI orchestrators who spawn/complete agents
76:   - Reason: Investigation 2025-12-25-design-orchestrator-completion-lifecycle-two incorrectly framed Dylan as the actor who spawns agents. The actor model is: Dylan ↔ AI Orchestrator ↔ Worker Agents. Mental model sync flows: Agent→Orchestrator (synthesis) and Orchestrator→Dylan (conversation).
77: - Investigation filenames are generated from task description at spawn time
78:   - Reason: generateSlug() runs at spawn before agent starts - cannot reflect findings in initial filename
79: - Complete agents before spawning more - maintain spawn:complete ratio
80:   - Reason: Dec 25 reflection: 201 issues created vs 12 closed in 24h. Backlog grew faster than completion. 29 stuck agents accumulated. Spawn discipline prevents debt accumulation.
81: 
82: ### Prior Decisions
83: - orch-go tmux spawn is fire-and-forget - no session ID capture
84:   - Reason: opencode run --attach is TUI-based; --format json gives session ID but loses TUI. Accept title-matching via orch status for monitoring.
85: - Headless Swarm = batch execution + rate-limit management across accounts
86:   - Reason: User clarified scope: focus on concurrent agent spawning with capacity management, not distributed architecture or multi-model routing
87: - orch spawn context delivery is reliable
88:   - Reason: Verified that SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is correctly populated and accessible by the agent
89: - Cross-project epics use Option A: epic in primary repo, ad-hoc spawns with --no-track in secondary repos, manual bd close with commit refs
90:   - Reason: Only working pattern today. Beads multi-repo hydration is read-only aggregation, bd repo commands are buggy.
91: - Tmux spawn uses opencode attach mode
92:   - Reason: Enables dual TUI+API access - sessions visible via orch status while still showing TUI for visual monitoring
93: - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is 100% redundant - generated from beads + kb context + skill + template
94:   - Reason: Investigation confirmed all content exists elsewhere and can be regenerated at spawn time
95: - Progressive disclosure for skill bloat
96:   - Reason: 89% of feature-impl spawns use only 2-3 phases. Extract detailed phase guidance to reference docs, keep core workflow inline. Proven pattern from 2025-11-21 instruction optimization. Reduces 1757→~500 lines.
97: - CompletionService bridges SSE completion detection and slot management for headless agents
98:   - Reason: Headless agents need automatic slot release when they complete; Monitor detects completions, WorkerPool manages slots, CompletionService links them
99: - Tiered spawn protocol uses .tier file in workspace for orch complete
100:   - Reason: Allows VerifyCompletion to read tier from workspace and skip SYNTHESIS.md requirement for light-tier spawns without requiring orchestrator to pass tier explicitly
101: - When spawned for cross-repo work, verify work completion status before starting
102:   - Reason: Task orch-go-oo1f: spawned in orch-go for work in orch-knowledge. Template was already retired (commit 7430185) before agent fully engaged. Quick verification could have saved agent context.
103: 
104: ### Models (synthesized understanding)
105: - Model Access and Spawn Paths
106:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/model-access-spawn-paths.md
107:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
108:     This model was last updated 2026-01-12.
109:     Changed files: pkg/spawn/config.go, CLAUDE.md.
110:     Deleted files: ~/.claude/skills/meta/orchestrator/SKILL.md.
111:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
112:   - Summary:
113:     Anthropic restricts Opus 4.5 access via fingerprinting that blocks API usage but allows Claude Code CLI with Max subscription. This constraint forced a **dual spawn architecture**: primary path (OpenCode API + Sonnet/Flash, headless, high concurrency) and escape hatch (Claude CLI + Opus, tmux, crash-resistant). The escape hatch exists because critical infrastructure work (fixing the spawn system itself) can't depend on what might fail. Model choice now encodes reliability requirements, not just quality preferences.
114:     
115:     ---
116:   - Critical Invariants:
117:     1. **Never spawn OpenCode infrastructure work without --backend claude --tmux**
118:        - Violation: Agent kills itself mid-execution when server restarts
119:     
120:     2. **Infrastructure detection runs before model auto-selection**
121:        - Priority 2.5 (between explicit flags and model-based selection)
122:        - Ensures auto-apply happens even without explicit --backend
123:     
124:     3. **Opus only accessible via Claude CLI backend**
125:        - API requests to Opus fail with auth error
126:        - Fingerprinting checks more than headers (TLS, HTTP/2 frames, ordering)
127:     
128:     4. **Escape hatch provides true independence**
129:        - Claude CLI binary ≠ OpenCode server
130:        - Tmux session persists across service restarts
131:        - Different authentication path (Max subscription OAuth)
132:     
133:     ---
134:   - Why This Fails:
135:     ### Failure Mode 1: Zombie Agents (Jan 8, 2026)
136:     
137:     **Symptom:** Agents tracked in registry but never actually ran
138:     
139:     **Root Cause:** Spawning with `--model opus` before understanding auth gate
140:     - orch created registry entry
141:     - OpenCode session created
142:     - Anthropic rejected API request (fingerprinting)
143:     - Agent hung in "running" state
144:     - Consumed concurrency slot without doing work
145:     
146:     **Examples:**
147:     - orch-go-mo0ja, orch-go-pzi2i, orch-go-aoei0, orch-go-gd1gd, orch-go-lwc3o
148:     
149:     **Fix:** Never use `--model opus` without `--backend claude`
150:     
151:     ### Failure Mode 2: Header Injection Conflicts (Jan 8, 2026)
152:     
153:     **Symptom:** Gemini Flash spawns hung after attempting Opus bypass
154:     
155:     **Root Cause:** Injected Claude Code headers (`x-app: cli`, `anthropic-version`, etc.) into OpenCode's Anthropic provider
156:     - Bypassed Opus gate (didn't work)
157:     - Broke Gemini spawns (headers conflicted with Bun fetch/SDK)
158:     - System-wide impact from localized change
159:     
160:     **Lesson:** Fingerprinting is more sophisticated than headers alone
161:     
162:     ### Failure Mode 3: Infrastructure Work Kills Itself
163:     
164:     **Symptom:** Agent fixing OpenCode server crashes mid-execution
165:     
166:     **Root Cause:** Agent spawned via OpenCode API, agent's fix restarts OpenCode server, agent's session killed
167:     
168:     **Solution:** Infrastructure work detection auto-applies `--backend claude --tmux`
169:     
170:     **Why auto-detection matters:**
171:     - Humans forget to add flags manually
172:     - Task description might not mention "opencode" explicitly
173:     - Keyword scan catches common patterns
174:     - Escape hatch becomes invisible safety net
175:     
176:     ---
177:   - Your findings should confirm, contradict, or extend the claims above.

191: - Spawn Architecture
192:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/spawn-architecture.md
193:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
194:     This model was last updated 2026-01-12.
195:     Changed files: cmd/orch/spawn_cmd.go, pkg/spawn/context.go, pkg/spawn/config.go.
196:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
197:   - Summary:
198:     Spawn evolved through 5 phases from basic CLI integration to daemon-driven automation with triage friction. The architecture creates a workspace with SPAWN_CONTEXT.md embedding skill content + task description + kb context, then launches an OpenCode session. The tier system (light/full) determines whether SYNTHESIS.md is required at completion. Triage friction (`--bypass-triage` flag) intentionally makes manual spawns harder to encourage daemon-driven workflow.
199:     
200:     ---
201:   - Critical Invariants:
202:     1. **Workspace name = kebab-case task description** - Used for tmux window, directory name, session title
203:     2. **Beads ID required for phase reporting** - `--no-track` creates untracked IDs that can't report to beads
204:     3. **KB context uses --global flag** - Cross-repo constraints are essential
205:     4. **Skill content stripped for --no-track** - Beads instructions removed when not tracking
206:     5. **Session scoping is per-project** - `orch send` only works within same directory hash
207:     6. **Token estimation at 4 chars/token** - Warning at 100k, error at 150k
208:     
209:     ---
210:   - Why This Fails:
211:     ### Failure Mode 1: Cross-Project Spawn Sets Wrong Session Directory
212:     
213:     **Symptom:** `orch spawn --workdir /other/project` creates session with orchestrator's directory
214:     
215:     **Root cause:** Session directory is set from spawn caller's CWD, not `--workdir` target
216:     
217:     **Why it happens:**
218:     - OpenCode infers directory from process CWD
219:     - `--workdir` changes agent's working directory, not spawn process CWD
220:     - Session gets orchestrator directory, beads issue in orchestrator project
221:     
222:     **Impact:**
223:     - Sessions unfindable via directory filtering
224:     - Cross-project work tracking is split
225:     
226:     **Fix needed:** Pass explicit directory to OpenCode session creation
227:     
228:     ### Failure Mode 2: Token Limit Exceeded on Spawn
229:     
230:     **Symptom:** Spawn fails with "context too large" error
231:     
232:     **Root cause:** SPAWN_CONTEXT.md exceeds 150k token limit
233:     
234:     **Why it happens:**
235:     - Skill content (~10-40k tokens)
236:     - KB context can be large (30-50k tokens)
237:     - Task description minimal
238:     - Estimation: 4 chars/token
239:     
240:     **Fix (Dec 22):** Warning at 100k tokens, hard error at 150k with guidance
241:     
242:     ### Failure Mode 3: Daemon Spawns Blocked Issues
243:     
244:     **Symptom:** Daemon spawns issue that has blockers
245:     
246:     **Root cause:** Dependency checking missing in triage workflow
247:     
248:     **Why it happens:**
249:     - `bd ready` returns issues without blockers
250:     - Daemon spawns from `triage:ready` label (doesn't check dependencies)
251:     - Race condition: issue labeled before dependencies checked
252:     
253:     **Fix (Jan 3):** Dependency gating with `--force` override flag
254:     
255:     ---

340:   - Summary:
341:     The daemon is an **autonomous agent spawner** that operates in a **poll-spawn-complete cycle**: polls beads for `triage:ready` issues, infers skill from issue type, spawns within capacity limits, monitors for `Phase: Complete`, verifies and closes. The daemon operates **independently of orchestrators** - orchestrators triage (label issues ready), daemon spawns (batch processing), orchestrators synthesize (review completed work). Skill inference uses **issue type** (not labels) to map task→investigation, bug→systematic-debugging, etc. Capacity management uses **WorkerPool** with reconciliation against OpenCode to free stale slots.
342:     
343:     ---
344:   - Why This Fails:
345:     ### 1. Capacity Starvation
346:     
347:     **What happens:** Pool shows MaxAgents active, but `orch status` shows fewer actual agents running.
348:     
349:     **Root cause:** Spawn failures don't release slots. Agent spawned, counted against pool, but spawn fails (bad skill name, missing context, etc.) - slot never released.
350:     
351:     **Why detection is hard:** Pool only knows about attempts, not outcomes. No feedback loop from spawn failure to pool.
352:     
353:     **Fix:** Reconciliation with OpenCode. Query actual sessions, release slots for non-existent agents.
354:     
355:     **Prevention:** Spawn tracking with retry limits (`pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`).
356:     
357:     ### 2. Duplicate Spawns
358:     
359:     **What happens:** Same issue spawned multiple times by daemon on consecutive polls.
360:     
361:     **Root cause:** Spawn latency. Issue labeled `triage:ready` at poll N, daemon spawns, but spawn hasn't transitioned issue to `in_progress` by poll N+1. Daemon sees same issue still ready, spawns again.
362:     
363:     **Why detection is hard:** Race condition between poll interval (60s) and spawn transition time (variable).
364:     
365:     **Fix:** Spawn deduplication via tracking. Track spawned beads IDs in memory, skip on subsequent polls until status confirms transition.
366:     
367:     **Source:** `pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`
368:     
369:     ### 3. Skill Inference Mismatch
370:     
371:     **What happens:** Daemon spawns wrong skill for issue type.
372:     
373:     **Root cause:** Issue type doesn't match actual work needed. User creates `task` for implementation work (should be `feature`), daemon infers `investigation`.
374:     
375:     **Why detection is hard:** Type is set at creation, can't be changed by daemon. Daemon trusts type.
376:     
377:     **Fix:** Manual override. Orchestrator updates issue type before labeling `triage:ready`, or spawns manually with correct skill.
378:     
379:     **Prevention:** Better issue creation prompts, type validation, skill override via labels (future).
380:

694: ## Constitutional Objection Protocol
695: 
696: **Trigger:** You believe an instruction conflicts with constitutional values (safety, ethics, honesty, user wellbeing) but it's not a clear Hard Limit violation.
697: 
698: **This is DIFFERENT from operational escalation:**
699: 
700: | Type | Examples | Route |
701: |------|----------|-------|
702: | **Operational** | "I'm blocked", "Requirements unclear", "Need decision" | → Orchestrator |
703: | **Constitutional** | "This could harm users", "This feels deceptive", "Ethical concern" | → Human (bypasses orchestrator) |
704: 
705: **Protocol when you have a constitutional concern:**
706: 
707: 1. **Identify the value** - Which constitutional principle is at risk? (safety, honesty, user autonomy, avoiding harm)
708: 
709: 2. **Document it** - `bd comment <id> "CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: [value] - [specific concern]"`
710: 
711: 3. **Do NOT proceed** with the concerning component
712: 
713: 4. **Continue** with unrelated components if the task is separable
714: 
715: 5. **Wait for HUMAN review** - Do not accept orchestrator override on constitutional matters
716: 
717: **Why this bypasses orchestrator:**
718: 
719: Claude's constitution says Claude can refuse unethical instructions regardless of the principal hierarchy. Orchestrators are Claude instances - they cannot authorize constitutional violations any more than you can. Human judgment is required for genuine ethical edge cases.
720: 
721: **Examples:**
722: 
723: | Situation | Response |
724: |-----------|----------|
725: | "Add tracking pixel without disclosure" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: user autonomy - undisclosed tracking |
726: | "Make the unsubscribe button hard to find" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: honesty - dark pattern design |
727: | "Scrape competitor's user data" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: ethics - unauthorized data collection |
728: | "Build feature that targets vulnerable users" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: avoiding harm - exploitation risk |
729: 
730: **When it's NOT a constitutional concern:**
731: - Technical disagreements about implementation
732: - Preference for different architecture
733: - Belief that requirements are suboptimal
734: - Wanting more context before proceeding
735: 
736: These are operational - escalate to orchestrator normally.

852: ### Cross-Repo Issue Handoff
853: 
854: **When you discover an issue that belongs to a different repo**, you cannot create it directly — `bd create` only works in the current project directory, and shell sandboxing prevents `cd` to other repos.
855: 
856: **Instead, output a structured `CROSS_REPO_ISSUE` block** in your beads completion comment or SYNTHESIS.md. The orchestrator will pick this up during completion review and create the issue in the target repo.
857: 
858: **Format:**
859: ```
860: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
861:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/<target-repo>
862:   title: "<concise issue title>"
863:   type: bug|task|feature|question
864:   priority: 0-4
865:   description: "<1-3 sentences with context, evidence, and why it matters>"
866: ```
867: 
868: **Rules:**
869: - Use absolute or `~`-relative paths for `repo`
870: - Include enough context in `description` for the issue to stand alone (the orchestrator in the other repo won't have your session context)
871: - One block per issue — multiple issues get multiple blocks
872: - Report blocks in your `Phase: Complete` comment: `Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block for price-watch`
873: 
874: **Example:**
875: ```bash
876: bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Complete - Implemented token refresh. Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block below.
877: 
878: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
879:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/price-watch
880:   title: Fix ScsOauthClient concurrent token refresh
881:   type: bug
882:   priority: 2
883:   description: During orch-go token handling work, discovered price-watch ScsOauthClient has a race condition when multiple goroutines call RefreshToken simultaneously. No mutex protects the shared token state."
884: ```
885: 
886: ---
887: 
888: 
889: 
890: ## Session Complete Protocol
891: 
892: **When your work is done (all deliverables ready), complete in this EXACT order:**
893: 
894: {{if eq .Tier "light"}}
895: 
896: 1. Author/update `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml` in the workspace root.
897:    - Fill the pre-populated skeleton with the exact commands you ran, expectations you verified, and any manual steps still required.
898: 2. Run: `bd comment {{.BeadsID}} "Phase: Complete - "[1-2 sentence summary of deliverables]"` (report phase FIRST - before commit)
899: 3. **Verify all .kb/ files are committed:**
900:    - Run: `git status --porcelain` and check for any .kb/ files (investigations, probes, decisions, etc.)
901:    - If uncommitted .kb/ files exist: `git add .kb/ && git commit -m "knowledge artifacts from session"`
902:    - This ensures probe files in .kb/models/{name}/probes/ are not left behind
903: 4. Commit any remaining changes (including `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml`)
904: 5. Run: `/exit` to close the agent session
905: 
906: **Light Tier:** SYNTHESIS.md is NOT required for this spawn.
907: {{else}}
908: 
909: 1. Author/update `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml` in the workspace root.
910:    - Fill the pre-populated skeleton with the exact commands you ran, expectations you verified, and any manual steps still required.
911: 2. Run: `bd comment {{.BeadsID}} "Phase: Complete - "[1-2 sentence summary of deliverables]"` (report phase FIRST - before commit)
912: 3. Ensure SYNTHESIS.md is created with these required sections:
913:    - **`Plain-Language Summary`** (REQUIRED): 2-4 sentences in plain language describing what you built/found/decided and why it matters. This is the scaffolding the orchestrator uses during completion review — write it for a human who hasn't read your code. No jargon without explanation. No "implemented X" without saying what X does.
914:    - **`Verification Contract`**: Link to `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml` and key outcomes
915: 4. **Verify all .kb/ files are committed:**
916:    - Run: `git status --porcelain` and check for any .kb/ files (investigations, probes, decisions, etc.)
917:    - If uncommitted .kb/ files exist: `git add .kb/ && git commit -m "knowledge artifacts from session"`
918:    - This ensures probe files in .kb/models/{name}/probes/ are not left behind
919: 5. Commit all remaining changes (including SYNTHESIS.md and `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml`)
920: 6. Run: `/exit` to close the agent session
921:    {{end}}
922: 
923: **Why this order matters:** If the agent dies after commit but before reporting Phase: Complete, the orchestrator cannot detect completion. Reporting phase first ensures visibility even if the agent dies before committing.
924: 
925: **Work is NOT complete until Phase: Complete is reported.**
926: The orchestrator cannot close this issue until you report Phase: Complete.
927: 
928: **Verification gates (orchestrator-run):**
929: After you report Phase: Complete, the orchestrator runs `orch complete` with two human gates:
930: 
931: - Gate 1 (explain-back): explain what was built and why.
932: - Gate 2 (behavioral, Tier 1 only): confirm behavior was verified.
933:   Make your Phase summary and VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml clear enough to support both gates.

17: // DefaultServerURL is the default OpenCode server URL.
18: // Uses 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost to avoid IPv6 resolution issues on macOS.
19: // On macOS, localhost can resolve to IPv6 ::1 while the server binds to IPv4,
20: // causing "connection refused" errors.
21: const DefaultServerURL = "http://127.0.0.1:4096"
22: 
23: // DefaultHTTPTimeout is the default timeout for HTTP requests to the OpenCode API.
24: // This prevents hangs when OpenCode is in a bad state (e.g., redirect loop).
25: const DefaultHTTPTimeout = 10 * time.Second
26: 
27: // LargeScannerBufferSize is the buffer size for scanning JSON events from opencode output.
28: // OpenCode JSON events can be very large (especially tool outputs with file contents),
29: // so we use 1MB instead of the default 64KB (bufio.MaxScanTokenSize) to prevent ErrTooLong.
30: const LargeScannerBufferSize = 1024 * 1024 // 1MB
31: 
32: // Client handles OpenCode CLI interactions.
33: type Client struct {
34: 	ServerURL  string
35: 	httpClient *http.Client
36: }
37: 
38: // NewClient creates a new OpenCode client with default timeout.
39: func NewClient(serverURL string) *Client {
40: 	return &Client{
41: 		ServerURL: serverURL,
42: 		httpClient: &http.Client{
43: 			Timeout: DefaultHTTPTimeout,
44: 			// Limit redirects to prevent redirect loops from hanging
45: 			CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {
46: 				if len(via) >= 10 {
47: 					return fmt.Errorf("too many redirects (max 10)")
48: 				}
49: 				return nil
50: 			},
51: 		},
52: 	}
53: }
54: 
55: // NewClientWithTimeout creates a new OpenCode client with custom timeout.
56: func NewClientWithTimeout(serverURL string, timeout time.Duration) *Client {
57: 	return &Client{
58: 		ServerURL: serverURL,
59: 		httpClient: &http.Client{
60: 			Timeout: timeout,
61: 			CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {
62: 				if len(via) >= 10 {
63: 					return fmt.Errorf("too many redirects (max 10)")
64: 				}
65: 				return nil
66: 			},
67: 		},
68: 	}
69: }

71: func (c *Client) getOpencodeBin() string {
72: 	if bin := os.Getenv("OPENCODE_BIN"); bin != "" {
73: 		return bin
74: 	}
75: 	return "opencode"

78: // ParseEvent parses a JSON event from opencode output.
79: func ParseEvent(line string) (Event, error) {
80: 	var event Event
81: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &event); err != nil {
82: 		return event, err
83: 	}
84: 	return event, nil
85: }
86: 
87: // ExtractSessionID extracts session ID from opencode events.
88: // OpenCode includes sessionID at the top level of each event.
89: func ExtractSessionID(events []string) (string, error) {
90: 	for _, line := range events {
91: 		event, err := ParseEvent(line)
92: 		if err != nil {
93: 			continue
94: 		}
95: 		if event.SessionID != "" {
96: 			return event.SessionID, nil
97: 		}
98: 	}
99: 	return "", ErrNoSessionID
100: }
101: 
102: // ExtractSessionIDFromReader reads from a reader until it finds a session ID.
103: // Returns as soon as a session ID is found, leaving remaining data unread.
104: // This is useful for headless spawns where we need the session ID quickly
105: // but don't want to block waiting for the process to complete.
106: func ExtractSessionIDFromReader(r io.Reader) (string, error) {
107: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
108: 	// Use large buffer to handle OpenCode's potentially large JSON events
109: 	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, LargeScannerBufferSize), LargeScannerBufferSize)
110: 	for scanner.Scan() {
111: 		line := scanner.Text()
112: 		if line == "" {
113: 			continue
114: 		}
115: 		event, err := ParseEvent(line)
116: 		if err != nil {
117: 			// Skip non-JSON lines
118: 			continue
119: 		}
120: 		if event.SessionID != "" {
121: 			return event.SessionID, nil
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
125: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("scanner error: %w", err)
126: 	}
127: 	return "", ErrNoSessionID
128: }
129: 
130: // ProcessOutput processes the output from opencode command.
131: func ProcessOutput(r io.Reader) (*Result, error) {
132: 	result := &Result{}
133: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
134: 	// Use large buffer to handle OpenCode's potentially large JSON events
135: 	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, LargeScannerBufferSize), LargeScannerBufferSize)
136: 
137: 	for scanner.Scan() {
138: 		line := scanner.Text()
139: 		if line == "" {
140: 			continue
141: 		}

144: 		if err != nil {

147: 		}

150: 
151: 		// sessionID is at top level of each event - grab from first event that has it
152: 		if result.SessionID == "" && event.SessionID != "" {
153: 			result.SessionID = event.SessionID
154: 		}
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
158: 		return result, err
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	return result, nil
162: }

164: // ProcessOutputWithStreaming processes the output from opencode command
165: // and streams text content to the provided writer.
166: func ProcessOutputWithStreaming(r io.Reader, streamTo io.Writer) (*Result, error) {
167: 	result := &Result{}
168: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
169: 	// Use large buffer to handle OpenCode's potentially large JSON events
170: 	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, LargeScannerBufferSize), LargeScannerBufferSize)
171: 
172: 	for scanner.Scan() {
173: 		line := scanner.Text()
174: 		if line == "" {
175: 			continue
176: 		}
177: 
178: 		event, err := ParseEvent(line)
179: 		if err != nil {
180: 			// Skip non-JSON lines

182: 		}
183: 
184: 		result.Events = append(result.Events, event)
185: 
186: 		// sessionID is at top level of each event - grab from first event that has it
187: 		if result.SessionID == "" && event.SessionID != "" {
188: 			result.SessionID = event.SessionID
189: 		}
190: 
191: 		// Stream text content to output
192: 		if event.Type == "text" && event.Content != "" {
193: 			streamTo.Write([]byte(event.Content))
194: 		}
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
198: 		return result, err
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	return result, nil
202: }

204: // BuildSpawnCommand builds the opencode spawn command.
205: func (c *Client) BuildSpawnCommand(prompt, title, model string) *exec.Cmd {
206: 	args := []string{
207: 		"run",
208: 		"--attach", c.ServerURL,
209: 		"--format", "json",
210: 	}

212: 	// Add --model flag only if model is provided

215: 	}

217: 	args = append(args, "--title", title, prompt)
218: 	return exec.Command(c.getOpencodeBin(), args...)
219: }

222: func (c *Client) BuildAskCommand(sessionID, prompt string) *exec.Cmd {
223: 	args := []string{
224: 		"run",
225: 		"--attach", c.ServerURL,
226: 		"--session", sessionID,
227: 		"--format", "json",
228: 		prompt,

233: // SendMessageAsync sends a message to an existing session asynchronously.
234: // The model parameter is optional - if empty, OpenCode will use the default model.
235: // Model should be in "provider/modelID" format (e.g., "google/gemini-2.5-flash").
236: func (c *Client) SendMessageAsync(sessionID, content, model string) error {
237: 	payload := map[string]any{
238: 		"parts": []map[string]string{{"type": "text", "text": content}},
239: 		"agent": "build",
240: 	}
241: 	// Include model if provided (per-message model in OpenCode)
242: 	// OpenCode expects model as an object with providerID and modelID fields
243: 	if model != "" {
244: 		modelObj := parseModelSpec(model)
245: 		if modelObj != nil {
246: 			payload["model"] = modelObj
247: 		}
248: 	}
249: 	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
250: 	if err != nil {
251: 		return err
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID+"/prompt_async", bytes.NewReader(body))
255: 	if err != nil {
256: 		return err
257: 	}
258: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
259: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
260: 	if err != nil {
261: 		return err
262: 	}
263: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
264: 
265: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
266: 		respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
267: 		return fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
268: 	}
269: 	return nil
270: }

272: // parseModelSpec parses a model string in "provider/modelID" format
273: // and returns a map suitable for the OpenCode API.
274: // Returns nil if the model string is empty or invalid.
275: func parseModelSpec(model string) map[string]string {
276: 	if model == "" {
277: 		return nil
278: 	}
279: 	// Expected format: "provider/modelID" (e.g., "google/gemini-2.5-flash")
280: 	idx := strings.Index(model, "/")
281: 	if idx <= 0 || idx >= len(model)-1 {
282: 		// Invalid format - no slash or empty provider/model
283: 		return nil
284: 	}
285: 	return map[string]string{
286: 		"providerID": model[:idx],
287: 		"modelID":    model[idx+1:],
288: 	}
289: }

291: // ListSessions fetches all sessions from the OpenCode API.
292: // If directory is provided, it passes it via x-opencode-directory header.
293: func (c *Client) ListSessions(directory string) ([]Session, error) {
294: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session", nil)

321: // GetSession fetches a single session by ID from the OpenCode API.

373: // SessionExists checks if a session exists in OpenCode (in-memory).
374: // Returns true if the session is accessible via the API, false otherwise.
375: // NOTE: This returns true for any persisted session, not just actively running ones.
376: // For liveness checks, use IsSessionActive() instead.
377: func (c *Client) SessionExists(sessionID string) bool {
378: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID, nil)
379: 	if err != nil {
380: 		return false
381: 	}
382: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
383: 	if err != nil {
384: 		return false
385: 	}
386: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
387: 
388: 	// Session exists if we get 200 OK
389: 	return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
390: }

392: // WaitForSessionError listens for session.error events for a limited time.
393: // Returns the error message if detected, empty string if none, or an error on connection failure.
394: func (c *Client) WaitForSessionError(sessionID string, timeout time.Duration) (string, error) {
395: 	if timeout <= 0 {
396: 		return "", nil
397: 	}
398: 
399: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
400: 	defer cancel()
401: 
402: 	sseClient := NewSSEClient(c.ServerURL + "/event")
403: 	events := make(chan SSEEvent, 10)
404: 	errCh := make(chan error, 1)
405: 
406: 	go func() {
407: 		errCh <- sseClient.ConnectWithContext(ctx, events)
408: 		close(events)
409: 	}()
410: 
411: 	for {
412: 		select {
413: 		case <-ctx.Done():
414: 			return "", nil
415: 		case event, ok := <-events:
416: 			if !ok {
417: 				select {
418: 				case err := <-errCh:
419: 					if err != nil && ctx.Err() == nil {
420: 						return "", err
421: 					}
422: 				default:
423: 				}
424: 				return "", nil
425: 			}
426: 			if event.Event != "session.error" && event.Event != "" {
427: 				continue
428: 			}
429: 			sid, msg := ParseSessionError(event.Data)
430: 			if sid == sessionID && msg != "" {
431: 				return msg, nil
432: 			}
433: 		case err := <-errCh:
434: 			if err != nil && ctx.Err() == nil {
435: 				return "", err
436: 			}
437: 			return "", nil
438: 		}
439: 	}
440: }

442: // IsSessionActive checks if a session is actively running (updated within maxIdleTime).
443: // This is more reliable than SessionExists() for liveness detection because OpenCode
444: // persists sessions to disk, so SessionExists() returns true for historical sessions.
445: // maxIdleTime is the maximum time since last update to consider a session "active".
446: func (c *Client) IsSessionActive(sessionID string, maxIdleTime time.Duration) bool {
447: 	session, err := c.GetSession(sessionID)
448: 	if err != nil {
449: 		return false
450: 	}
451: 
452: 	updatedAt := time.Unix(session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
453: 	idleTime := time.Since(updatedAt)
454: 	return idleTime <= maxIdleTime
455: }
456: 
457: // IsSessionProcessing checks if a session is actively processing (has a pending assistant response).
458: // This is the most reliable signal for detecting truly active agents because it checks
459: // whether the last assistant message has finished (finish != "" and completed != 0).
460: // Returns true if the session is currently generating a response, false if idle.
461: func (c *Client) IsSessionProcessing(sessionID string) bool {
462: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
463: 	if err != nil || len(messages) == 0 {
464: 		return false
465: 	}
466: 
467: 	// Find the last message
468: 	lastMsg := messages[len(messages)-1]
469: 
470: 	// Session is processing if:
471: 	// 1. Last message is from assistant AND
472: 	// 2. It hasn't finished yet (finish is empty and completed is 0)
473: 	if lastMsg.Info.Role == "assistant" {
474: 		return lastMsg.Info.Finish == "" && lastMsg.Info.Time.Completed == 0
475: 	}
476: 
477: 	// If last message is from user, check if there's an assistant response being generated
478: 	// This happens when user sends a message and assistant hasn't started responding yet
479: 	// In this case, we consider the session as processing (waiting for response)
480: 	if lastMsg.Info.Role == "user" {
481: 		// If the user message was sent recently (within last 30 seconds), consider it processing
482: 		createdAt := time.Unix(lastMsg.Info.Time.Created/1000, 0)
483: 		return time.Since(createdAt) < 30*time.Second
484: 	}
485: 
486: 	return false
487: }
488: 
489: // GetLastMessage returns the last message in a session, or nil if the session has no messages.
490: func (c *Client) GetLastMessage(sessionID string) (*Message, error) {
491: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
492: 	if err != nil {
493: 		return nil, err
494: 	}
495: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
496: 		return nil, nil
497: 	}
498: 	return &messages[len(messages)-1], nil
499: }
500: 
501: // LastActivity represents the most recent activity from a session.
502: // This is used to populate agent activity on initial dashboard load.
503: type LastActivity struct {
504: 	Text      string // Activity description (truncated for display)
505: 	Timestamp int64  // Unix timestamp in milliseconds
506: }
507: 
508: // GetLastActivity extracts the last meaningful activity from session messages.
509: // It looks for the most recent assistant message and extracts a summary of what
510: // the agent is doing (tool use, text generation, etc.).
511: // Returns nil if no activity can be extracted.
512: func (c *Client) GetLastActivity(sessionID string) (*LastActivity, error) {
513: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
514: 	if err != nil {
515: 		return nil, err
516: 	}
517: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
518: 		return nil, nil
519: 	}
520: 
521: 	// Find the last assistant message (most relevant for activity)
522: 	var lastAssistantMsg *Message
523: 	for i := len(messages) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
524: 		if messages[i].Info.Role == "assistant" {
525: 			lastAssistantMsg = &messages[i]
526: 			break
527: 		}
528: 	}
529: 
530: 	if lastAssistantMsg == nil {
531: 		return nil, nil
532: 	}
533: 
534: 	// Extract activity from message parts
535: 	// Priority: tool invocation > text > reasoning
536: 	var activityText string
537: 	for _, part := range lastAssistantMsg.Parts {
538: 		switch part.Type {
539: 		case "tool-invocation", "tool":
540: 			// Tool use is the most informative activity
541: 			activityText = "Using tool: " + extractToolName(part.Text)
542: 			break
543: 		case "text":
544: 			if part.Text != "" && activityText == "" {
545: 				// Truncate long text
546: 				activityText = truncateText(part.Text, 80)
547: 			}
548: 		case "reasoning":
549: 			if activityText == "" {
550: 				activityText = "Thinking..."
551: 			}
552: 		}
553: 	}
554: 
555: 	if activityText == "" {
556: 		return nil, nil
557: 	}
558: 
559: 	// Use message completion time if available, otherwise created time
560: 	timestamp := lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Completed
561: 	if timestamp == 0 {
562: 		timestamp = lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Created
563: 	}
564: 
565: 	return &LastActivity{
566: 		Text:      activityText,
567: 		Timestamp: timestamp,
568: 	}, nil
569: }
570: 
571: // extractToolName tries to extract a tool name from tool invocation text.
572: // Tool text can be JSON or plain text depending on OpenCode version.
573: func extractToolName(text string) string {
574: 	// Try to extract tool name - the text might be structured or plain
575: 	if text == "" {
576: 		return "unknown"
577: 	}
578: 	// Truncate for display
579: 	if len(text) > 50 {
580: 		return text[:50] + "..."
581: 	}
582: 	return text
583: }
584: 
585: // truncateText truncates text to maxLen characters with ellipsis.
586: func truncateText(text string, maxLen int) string {
587: 	if len(text) <= maxLen {
588: 		return text
589: 	}
590: 	// Find last space before maxLen to avoid cutting words
591: 	for i := maxLen - 3; i > 0; i-- {
592: 		if text[i] == ' ' {
593: 			return text[:i] + "..."
594: 		}
595: 	}
596: 	return text[:maxLen-3] + "..."
597: }

599: // CreateSessionRequest represents the request body for creating a new session.
600: type CreateSessionRequest struct {
601: 	Title     string            `json:"title,omitempty"`
602: 	Directory string            `json:"directory,omitempty"`
603: 	Model     string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
604: 	Metadata  map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
605: 	TimeTTL   int               `json:"time_ttl,omitempty"` // Session TTL in seconds (0 = no expiration)
606: }
607: 
608: // CreateSessionResponse represents the response from creating a new session.
609: type CreateSessionResponse struct {
610: 	ID        string `json:"id"`
611: 	Title     string `json:"title,omitempty"`
612: 	Directory string `json:"directory,omitempty"`
613: }
614: 
615: // CreateSession creates a new OpenCode session via HTTP API.
616: // This is used for headless spawns (no tmux window).
617: // timeTTL is the session expiration time in seconds (0 = no expiration).
618: func (c *Client) CreateSession(title, directory, model string, metadata map[string]string, timeTTL int) (*CreateSessionResponse, error) {
619: 	payload := CreateSessionRequest{
620: 		Title:     title,
621: 		Directory: directory,
622: 		Model:     model,
623: 		Metadata:  metadata,
624: 		TimeTTL:   timeTTL,
625: 	}
626: 	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
627: 	if err != nil {
628: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal request: %w", err)
629: 	}
630: 
631: 	// Create request to set custom headers
632: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session", bytes.NewReader(body))
633: 	if err != nil {
634: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
635: 	}
636: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
637: 
638: 	// OpenCode expects directory via x-opencode-directory header
639: 	if directory != "" {
640: 		req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", directory)
641: 	}
642: 
643: 	// Set ORCH_WORKER=1 header to signal this is an orch-managed worker session
644: 	// This allows the session-context plugin to skip loading orchestrator skill
645: 	req.Header.Set("x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER", "1")
646: 
647: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
648: 	if err != nil {
649: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create session: %w", err)
650: 	}
651: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
652: 
653: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
654: 		respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
655: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
656: 	}
657: 
658: 	var result CreateSessionResponse
659: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&result); err != nil {
660: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode response: %w", err)
661: 	}
662: 
663: 	return &result, nil
664: }

673: // SendMessageInDirectory sends a message to a session with the x-opencode-directory header.

676: func (c *Client) SendMessageInDirectory(sessionID, content, model, directory string) error {

683: 		if modelObj != nil {

685: 		}
686: 	}

688: 	if err != nil {

690: 	}

692: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID+"/prompt_async", bytes.NewReader(body))
693: 	if err != nil {

696: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")

700: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
701: 	if err != nil {
702: 		return err
703: 	}
704: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
705: 
706: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
707: 		respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
708: 		return fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
709: 	}

713: // WaitForSessionIdle polls GET /session/status and blocks until the specified session
714: // transitions from busy to idle. Used by inline spawn mode to maintain blocking
715: // behavior while using the HTTP API for session creation.
716: //
717: // Replaced SSE-based implementation with polling for simplicity. Polls every 500ms.
718: func (c *Client) WaitForSessionIdle(sessionID string) error {
719: 	const pollInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
720: 	const maxWait = 10 * time.Minute // Safety timeout
721: 
722: 	deadline := time.Now().Add(maxWait)
723: 	var sessionWasBusy bool
724: 
725: 	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
726: 		status, err := c.GetSessionStatusByID(sessionID)
727: 		if err != nil {
728: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get session status: %w", err)
729: 		}
730: 
731: 		// Track if session was ever busy
732: 		if status.IsBusy() || status.IsRetrying() {
733: 			sessionWasBusy = true
734: 		}
735: 
736: 		// Completion: session was busy and is now idle
737: 		if sessionWasBusy && status.IsIdle() {
738: 			return nil
739: 		}
740: 
741: 		// Poll again after interval
742: 		time.Sleep(pollInterval)
743: 	}
744: 
745: 	return fmt.Errorf("timeout waiting for session %s to complete (max %v)", sessionID, maxWait)
746: }

748: // GetMessages fetches all messages for a session from the OpenCode API.

772: // FindRecentSession finds the most recent session for a given project directory.
773: // It matches by directory and creation time only (within 30 seconds).
774: // Title matching is not used because OpenCode session titles are set to the first
775: // prompt text, not the workspace name, making title matching unreliable.
776: func (c *Client) FindRecentSession(projectDir string) (string, error) {
777: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session", nil)
778: 	if err != nil {
779: 		return "", err
780: 	}
781: 	req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", projectDir)
782: 
783: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
784: 	if err != nil {
785: 		return "", err
786: 	}
787: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
788: 
789: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
790: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d", resp.StatusCode)
791: 	}
792: 
793: 	var sessions []Session
794: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&sessions); err != nil {
795: 		return "", err
796: 	}
797: 
798: 	// Find the most recent session for this directory (within 30 seconds)
799: 	var mostRecent *Session
800: 	now := time.Now().UnixMilli()
801: 	for i := range sessions {
802: 		s := &sessions[i]
803: 		if s.Directory != projectDir {
804: 			continue
805: 		}
806: 		// Only match sessions created in the last 30 seconds
807: 		if now-s.Time.Created > 30*1000 {
808: 			continue
809: 		}
810: 		if mostRecent == nil || s.Time.Created > mostRecent.Time.Created {
811: 			mostRecent = s
812: 		}
813: 	}
814: 
815: 	if mostRecent == nil {
816: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("no sessions found for directory: %s (within last 30s)", projectDir)
817: 	}
818: 
819: 	return mostRecent.ID, nil
820: }
821: 
822: // FindRecentSessionWithRetry retries finding a recent session with exponential backoff.
823: // This handles the race condition where OpenCode TUI starts before registering with the API.
824: // Returns the session ID if found, or empty string with error if not found after retries.
825: func (c *Client) FindRecentSessionWithRetry(projectDir string, maxAttempts int, initialDelay time.Duration) (string, error) {
826: 	delay := initialDelay
827: 	var lastErr error
828: 
829: 	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++ {
830: 		sessionID, err := c.FindRecentSession(projectDir)
831: 		if err == nil {
832: 			return sessionID, nil
833: 		}
834: 		lastErr = err
835: 
836: 		// Don't sleep after the last attempt
837: 		if attempt < maxAttempts {
838: 			time.Sleep(delay)
839: 			delay = delay * 2 // Exponential backoff
840: 		}
841: 	}
842: 
843: 	// Return empty string (not an error) since tmux window_id is sufficient for monitoring
844: 	return "", lastErr
845: }
846: 
847: // ExtractRecentText extracts the most recent text content from messages.
848: // It returns up to `lines` worth of text from the most recent messages.
849: // The text is extracted from message parts of type "text".
850: func ExtractRecentText(messages []Message, lines int) []string {
851: 	var result []string
852: 
853: 	// Process messages in reverse order (most recent first)
854: 	for i := len(messages) - 1; i >= 0 && len(result) < lines; i-- {
855: 		msg := messages[i]
856: 
857: 		// Extract text from parts
858: 		for j := len(msg.Parts) - 1; j >= 0 && len(result) < lines; j-- {
859: 			part := msg.Parts[j]
860: 			if part.Type == "text" && part.Text != "" {
861: 				// Split text into lines and add in reverse
862: 				textLines := strings.Split(part.Text, "\n")
863: 				for k := len(textLines) - 1; k >= 0 && len(result) < lines; k-- {
864: 					line := textLines[k]
865: 					if line != "" || len(result) > 0 { // Skip leading empty lines
866: 						result = append([]string{line}, result...)
867: 					}
868: 				}
869: 			}
870: 		}
871: 	}
872: 
873: 	// Trim to requested line count
874: 	if len(result) > lines {
875: 		result = result[len(result)-lines:]
876: 	}
877: 
878: 	return result
879: }

915: // DeleteSession deletes an OpenCode session by ID.
916: // Returns nil on success, error on failure.
917: func (c *Client) DeleteSession(sessionID string) error {
918: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("DELETE", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID, nil)
919: 	if err != nil {
920: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
921: 	}
922: 
923: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
924: 	if err != nil {
925: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to delete session: %w", err)
926: 	}
927: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
928: 
929: 	// Accept 200 OK or 204 No Content as success

938: // SendMessageWithStreaming sends a message to a session and streams the response.
939: // It sends the message via the async API, then connects to SSE to stream text events
940: // until the session becomes idle. Text content is written to the provided writer.
941: func (c *Client) SendMessageWithStreaming(sessionID, content string, streamTo io.Writer) error {
942: 	// Send the message via async API first (no model specified for Q&A)
943: 	if err := c.SendMessageAsync(sessionID, content, ""); err != nil {
944: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send message: %w", err)
945: 	}
946: 
947: 	// Connect to SSE and stream the response
948: 	// Use a client without timeout for SSE - it's a long-running stream
949: 	sseClient := &http.Client{
950: 		// No timeout - SSE is meant to be long-running
951: 		// But still limit redirects
952: 		CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {
953: 			if len(via) >= 10 {
954: 				return fmt.Errorf("too many redirects (max 10)")
955: 			}
956: 			return nil
957: 		},
958: 	}
959: 	sseURL := c.ServerURL + "/event"
960: 	resp, err := sseClient.Get(sseURL)
961: 	if err != nil {
962: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to connect to SSE: %w", err)
963: 	}
964: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
965: 
966: 	reader := bufio.NewReader(resp.Body)
967: 	var eventBuffer strings.Builder
968: 	var sessionWasBusy bool
969: 	var messageIDSeen = make(map[string]bool) // Track message IDs to avoid duplicates
970: 
971: 	for {
972: 		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
973: 		if err != nil {
974: 			if err == io.EOF {
975: 				return nil
976: 			}
977: 			return err
978: 		}
979: 
980: 		eventBuffer.WriteString(line)
981: 
982: 		// Empty line signals end of event
983: 		if line == "\n" && eventBuffer.Len() > 1 {
984: 			raw := eventBuffer.String()
985: 			eventType, data := ParseSSEEvent(raw)
986: 			eventBuffer.Reset()
987: 
988: 			if data == "" {
989: 				continue
990: 			}
991: 
992: 			// Parse the JSON data
993: 			var eventData map[string]interface{}
994: 			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &eventData); err != nil {
995: 				continue
996: 			}
997: 
998: 			// Check if this event is for our session
999: 			eventSessionID := ""
1000: 			if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1001: 				if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok {
1002: 					eventSessionID = sid
1003: 				}
1004: 			}
1005: 			// Also check top-level sessionID
1006: 			if sid, ok := eventData["sessionID"].(string); ok && eventSessionID == "" {
1007: 				eventSessionID = sid
1008: 			}
1009: 
1010: 			// Skip events from other sessions
1011: 			if eventSessionID != "" && eventSessionID != sessionID {
1012: 				continue
1013: 			}
1014: 
1015: 			// Handle session error events
1016: 			if eventType == "session.error" {
1017: 				if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1018: 					// Check if this error is for our session
1019: 					if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid == sessionID {
1020: 						// Extract error details
1021: 						if errorObj, ok := props["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1022: 							if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
1023: 								return fmt.Errorf("session error: %s", msg)
1024: 							}
1025: 						}
1026: 						return fmt.Errorf("session error occurred")
1027: 					}
1028: 				}
1029: 				// Also check top-level sessionID for older format
1030: 				if sid, ok := eventData["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid == sessionID {
1031: 					if errorObj, ok := eventData["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1032: 						if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
1033: 							return fmt.Errorf("session error: %s", msg)
1034: 						}
1035: 					}
1036: 					return fmt.Errorf("session error occurred")
1037: 				}
1038: 				continue
1039: 			}
1040: 
1041: 			// Handle session status events
1042: 			if eventType == "session.status" {
1043: 				status, sid := ParseSessionStatus(data)
1044: 				if sid == sessionID {
1045: 					if status == "busy" || status == "running" {
1046: 						sessionWasBusy = true
1047: 					}
1048: 					// Completion: session was busy and is now idle
1049: 					if sessionWasBusy && status == "idle" {
1050: 						return nil
1051: 					}
1052: 				}
1053: 				continue
1054: 			}
1055: 
1056: 			// Handle message.part events (text streaming)
1057: 			if eventType == "message.part" {
1058: 				if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1059: 					// Check this is for our session
1060: 					if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid != sessionID {
1061: 						continue
1062: 					}
1063: 
1064: 					// Get message ID to track what we've seen
1065: 					messageID := ""
1066: 					if mid, ok := props["messageID"].(string); ok {
1067: 						messageID = mid
1068: 					}
1069: 
1070: 					// Get the part data
1071: 					if part, ok := props["part"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1072: 						if partType, ok := part["type"].(string); ok && partType == "text" {
1073: 							if text, ok := part["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
1074: 								// Write text to output
1075: 								streamTo.Write([]byte(text))
1076: 							}
1077: 						}
1078: 					}
1079: 
1080: 					// Track message ID if provided
1081: 					if messageID != "" {
1082: 						messageIDSeen[messageID] = true
1083: 					}
1084: 				}
1085: 			}
1086: 		}
1087: 	}
1088: }
1089: 
1090: // TokenStats represents aggregated token usage for a session.
1091: type TokenStats struct {
1092: 	InputTokens     int `json:"input_tokens"`
1093: 	OutputTokens    int `json:"output_tokens"`
1094: 	ReasoningTokens int `json:"reasoning_tokens,omitempty"`
1095: 	CacheReadTokens int `json:"cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
1096: 	TotalTokens     int `json:"total_tokens"` // input + output + reasoning
1097: }
1098: 
1099: // AggregateTokens calculates total token usage from a slice of messages.
1100: // It sums up input, output, reasoning, and cache tokens across all messages.
1101: func AggregateTokens(messages []Message) TokenStats {
1102: 	var stats TokenStats
1103: 	for _, msg := range messages {
1104: 		if msg.Info.Tokens == nil {
1105: 			continue
1106: 		}
1107: 		stats.InputTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Input
1108: 		stats.OutputTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Output
1109: 		stats.ReasoningTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Reasoning
1110: 		if msg.Info.Tokens.Cache != nil {
1111: 			stats.CacheReadTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read
1112: 		}
1113: 	}
1114: 	stats.TotalTokens = stats.InputTokens + stats.OutputTokens + stats.ReasoningTokens
1115: 	return stats
1116: }
1117: 
1118: // GetSessionTokens fetches messages for a session and returns aggregated token stats.
1119: // Returns nil if session doesn't exist or has no messages.
1120: func (c *Client) GetSessionTokens(sessionID string) (*TokenStats, error) {
1121: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
1122: 	if err != nil {
1123: 		return nil, err
1124: 	}
1125: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
1126: 		return nil, nil
1127: 	}
1128: 	stats := AggregateTokens(messages)
1129: 	return &stats, nil
1130: }
1131: 
1132: // GetAllSessionStatus fetches the status of all sessions from GET /session/status endpoint.
1133: // Returns a map of sessionID -> SessionStatusInfo.
1134: // Sessions not in the map are considered idle (status is in-memory only in OpenCode).
1135: func (c *Client) GetAllSessionStatus() (map[string]SessionStatusInfo, error) {
1136: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session/status", nil)
1137: 	if err != nil {
1138: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
1139: 	}
1140: 
1141: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
1142: 	if err != nil {
1143: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch session status: %w", err)
1144: 	}
1145: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
1146: 
1147: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
1148: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d", resp.StatusCode)
1149: 	}
1150: 
1151: 	var result map[string]SessionStatusInfo
1152: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&result); err != nil {
1153: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode session status: %w", err)
1154: 	}
1155: 
1156: 	return result, nil
1157: }
1158: 
1159: // GetSessionStatusByID fetches the status of a specific session.
1160: // Returns nil if session is idle (status deleted from in-memory map in OpenCode).
1161: // Use SessionExists() to distinguish between "session doesn't exist" vs "session is idle".
1162: func (c *Client) GetSessionStatusByID(sessionID string) (*SessionStatusInfo, error) {
1163: 	allStatus, err := c.GetAllSessionStatus()
1164: 	if err != nil {
1165: 		return nil, err
1166: 	}
1167: 
1168: 	status, exists := allStatus[sessionID]
1169: 	if !exists {
1170: 		// Session not in status map = idle (or doesn't exist)
1171: 		return &SessionStatusInfo{Type: "idle"}, nil
1172: 	}
1173: 
1174: 	return &status, nil
1175: }
1176: 
1177: // ExportSessionTranscript fetches all messages for a session and formats them as markdown.
1178: // This is useful for preserving conversation history before deleting a session.
1179: // Returns the markdown transcript and any error encountered.
1180: func (c *Client) ExportSessionTranscript(sessionID string) (string, error) {
1181: 	// Get session info
1182: 	session, err := c.GetSession(sessionID)
1183: 	if err != nil {
1184: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get session: %w", err)
1185: 	}
1186: 
1187: 	// Get all messages
1188: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
1189: 	if err != nil {
1190: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get messages: %w", err)
1191: 	}
1192: 
1193: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
1194: 		return "", nil // No messages to export
1195: 	}
1196: 
1197: 	return FormatMessagesAsTranscript(session, messages), nil
1198: }
1199: 
1200: // FormatMessagesAsTranscript converts session info and messages to a markdown transcript.
1201: func FormatMessagesAsTranscript(session *Session, messages []Message) string {
1202: 	var lines []string
1203: 
1204: 	// Header
1205: 	lines = append(lines, "# Session Transcript", "")
1206: 
1207: 	// Session metadata
1208: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Title:** %s", session.Title))
1209: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Session ID:** `%s`", session.ID))
1210: 	if session.Directory != "" {
1211: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Directory:** `%s`", session.Directory))
1212: 	}
1213: 	if session.Time.Created > 0 {
1214: 		t := time.Unix(session.Time.Created/1000, 0)
1215: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Started:** %s", t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")))
1216: 	}
1217: 	if session.Time.Updated > 0 {
1218: 		t := time.Unix(session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
1219: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Updated:** %s", t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")))
1220: 	}
1221: 
1222: 	// Summary stats
1223: 	if session.Summary.Additions > 0 || session.Summary.Deletions > 0 || session.Summary.Files > 0 {
1224: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Changes:** +%d/-%d in %d files",
1225: 			session.Summary.Additions, session.Summary.Deletions, session.Summary.Files))
1226: 	}
1227: 
1228: 	lines = append(lines, "", "---", "")
1229: 
1230: 	// Format messages
1231: 	for _, msg := range messages {
1232: 		formatted := formatMessageToMarkdown(&msg)
1233: 		if formatted != "" {
1234: 			lines = append(lines, formatted)
1235: 		}
1236: 	}
1237: 
1238: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
1239: }
1240: 
1241: // formatMessageToMarkdown formats a single message to markdown.
1242: func formatMessageToMarkdown(msg *Message) string {
1243: 	// Collect text parts and tool parts
1244: 	var textParts []string
1245: 	var toolParts []MessagePart
1246: 
1247: 	for _, part := range msg.Parts {
1248: 		switch part.Type {
1249: 		case "text":
1250: 			text := strings.TrimSpace(part.Text)
1251: 			if text != "" {
1252: 				textParts = append(textParts, text)
1253: 			}
1254: 		case "tool", "tool-invocation":
1255: 			toolParts = append(toolParts, part)
1256: 		}
1257: 	}
1258: 
1259: 	// Skip message if no content
1260: 	if len(textParts) == 0 && len(toolParts) == 0 {
1261: 		return ""
1262: 	}
1263: 
1264: 	var lines []string
1265: 
1266: 	// Header with role and timestamp
1267: 	var timestamp string
1268: 	if msg.Info.Time.Created > 0 {
1269: 		t := time.Unix(msg.Info.Time.Created/1000, 0)
1270: 		timestamp = t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")
1271: 	}
1272: 
1273: 	switch msg.Info.Role {
1274: 	case "user":
1275: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## User (%s)", timestamp))
1276: 	case "assistant":
1277: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## Assistant (%s)", timestamp))
1278: 		// Add token/cost info
1279: 		if msg.Info.Tokens != nil {
1280: 			var tokenInfo []string
1281: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Input > 0 {
1282: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("in:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Input))
1283: 			}
1284: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Output > 0 {
1285: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("out:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Output))
1286: 			}
1287: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Cache != nil && msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read > 0 {
1288: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("cached:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read))
1289: 			}
1290: 			if msg.Info.Cost > 0 {
1291: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("$%.4f", msg.Info.Cost))
1292: 			}
1293: 			if len(tokenInfo) > 0 {
1294: 				lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("*Tokens: %s*", strings.Join(tokenInfo, ", ")))
1295: 			}
1296: 		}
1297: 	default:
1298: 		role := msg.Info.Role
1299: 		if role != "" {
1300: 			role = strings.ToUpper(role[:1]) + role[1:]
1301: 		}
1302: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## %s (%s)", role, timestamp))
1303: 	}
1304: 
1305: 	lines = append(lines, "")
1306: 
1307: 	// Add text content
1308: 	for _, text := range textParts {
1309: 		lines = append(lines, text, "")
1310: 	}
1311: 
1312: 	// Add tool summaries
1313: 	if len(toolParts) > 0 {
1314: 		lines = append(lines, "**Tools:**")
1315: 		for _, tool := range toolParts {
1316: 			// Format tool details for better debugging
1317: 			toolDesc := formatToolDescription(&tool)
1318: 			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  - %s", toolDesc))
1319: 		}
1320: 		lines = append(lines, "")
1321: 	}
1322: 
1323: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
1324: }
1325: 
1326: // formatToolDescription formats a tool part into a human-readable string.
1327: // Shows tool name, title/description, and key parameters for debugging.
1328: func formatToolDescription(tool *MessagePart) string {
1329: 	// If no tool details available, fall back to type
1330: 	if tool.Tool == "" {
1331: 		return tool.Type
1332: 	}
1333: 
1334: 	// Start with tool name
1335: 	result := tool.Tool
1336: 
1337: 	// Add title/description if available (most useful for bash commands)
1338: 	if tool.State != nil && tool.State.Title != "" {
1339: 		result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, tool.State.Title)
1340: 	} else if tool.State != nil && len(tool.State.Input) > 0 {
1341: 		// If no title, try to extract a useful parameter
1342: 		// For common tools, show the most relevant parameter
1343: 		switch tool.Tool {
1344: 		case "read":
1345: 			if filePath, ok := tool.State.Input["filePath"].(string); ok {
1346: 				// Show just filename, not full path, to keep it concise
1347: 				filename := filePath
1348: 				if idx := strings.LastIndex(filePath, "/"); idx >= 0 && idx < len(filePath)-1 {
1349: 					filename = filePath[idx+1:]
1350: 				}
1351: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, filename)
1352: 			}
1353: 		case "edit", "write":
1354: 			if filePath, ok := tool.State.Input["filePath"].(string); ok {
1355: 				filename := filePath
1356: 				if idx := strings.LastIndex(filePath, "/"); idx >= 0 && idx < len(filePath)-1 {
1357: 					filename = filePath[idx+1:]
1358: 				}
1359: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, filename)
1360: 			}
1361: 		case "bash":
1362: 			if command, ok := tool.State.Input["command"].(string); ok {
1363: 				// Truncate long commands to 60 chars
1364: 				if len(command) > 60 {
1365: 					command = command[:57] + "..."
1366: 				}
1367: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, command)
1368: 			}
1369: 		case "grep", "glob":
1370: 			if pattern, ok := tool.State.Input["pattern"].(string); ok {
1371: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, pattern)
1372: 			}
1373: 		default:
1374: 			// For other tools, just show the tool name
1375: 		}
1376: 	}
1377: 
1378: 	return result
1379: }
1380:

90: // InferSkillFromIssueType maps issue types to appropriate skills.

92: //

95: // most bugs reported as vague symptoms need understanding before patching.
96: // Use explicit skill:systematic-debugging label for isolated bugs with clear cause.
97: func InferSkillFromIssueType(issueType string) (string, error) {
98: 	switch issueType {
99: 	case "bug":
100: 		// Default to architect: understand before fixing
101: 		// Use skill:systematic-debugging label for clear, isolated bugs
102: 		return "architect", nil
103: 	case "feature":
104: 		return "feature-impl", nil
105: 	case "task":
106: 		return "feature-impl", nil
107: 	case "investigation":

109: 	case "epic":
110: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("cannot spawn work on epic issues - epics are decomposed into sub-issues")
111: 	case "":
112: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("issue type is empty")
113: 	default:

115: 	}

118: // DetermineSpawnTier determines the spawn tier based on flags, config, task scope signals,
119: // and skill defaults.
120: // Priority: --light flag > --full flag > userconfig.default_tier > task scope signals > skill default
121: func DetermineSpawnTier(skillName, task string, lightFlag, fullFlag bool) string {
122: 	// Explicit flags take precedence
123: 	if lightFlag {
124: 		return spawn.TierLight
125: 	}
126: 	if fullFlag {
127: 		return spawn.TierFull
128: 	}
129: 	// Check userconfig for default tier override
130: 	cfg, err := userconfig.Load()
131: 	if err == nil && cfg.GetDefaultTier() != "" {
132: 		return cfg.GetDefaultTier()
133: 	}
134: 	// Use task scope signals to upgrade to full tier when needed
135: 	if inferredTier := inferTierFromTask(task); inferredTier != "" {
136: 		return inferredTier
137: 	}
138: 	// Fall back to skill default
139: 	return spawn.DefaultTierForSkill(skillName)
140: }

142: func inferTierFromTask(task string) string {
143: 	if task == "" {
144: 		return ""
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	if scope := parseSessionScope(task); scope != "" {
148: 		switch scope {
149: 		case "medium", "large", "full", "4-6h", "4-6h+", "2-4h":
150: 			return spawn.TierFull
151: 		}
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	lower := strings.ToLower(task)
155: 
156: 	score := 0
157: 	if containsAny(lower, []string{
158: 		"create package",
159: 		"new package",
160: 		"create module",
161: 		"new module",
162: 		"new pkg/",
163: 		"create pkg/",
164: 		"new package/",
165: 		"create package/",
166: 	}) {
167: 		score += 2
168: 	}
169: 	if containsAny(lower, []string{
170: 		"comprehensive tests",
171: 		"test suite",
172: 		"integration tests",
173: 		"unit tests",
174: 		"tests for",
175: 		"add tests",
176: 	}) {
177: 		score++
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	if score >= 2 {
181: 		return spawn.TierFull
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	return ""
185: }

187: func parseSessionScope(task string) string {
188: 	matches := sessionScopeRegex.FindStringSubmatch(task)
189: 	if len(matches) < 2 {
190: 		return ""
191: 	}
192: 	scope := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(matches[1]))
193: 	if scope == "" {
194: 		return ""
195: 	}
196: 	fields := strings.Fields(scope)
197: 	if len(fields) == 0 {
198: 		return ""
199: 	}
200: 	return fields[0]
201: }

203: func containsAny(text string, terms []string) bool {
204: 	for _, term := range terms {
205: 		if strings.Contains(text, term) {
206: 			return true
207: 		}
208: 	}
209: 	return false
210: }

212: // CheckAndAutoSwitchAccount checks if the current account is over usage thresholds
213: // and automatically switches to a better account if available.
214: // Returns nil if no switch was needed or switch succeeded.
215: // Logs the switch action if one occurs.
216: func CheckAndAutoSwitchAccount() error {
217: 	// Get thresholds from environment or use defaults
218: 	thresholds := account.DefaultAutoSwitchThresholds()
219: 
220: 	// Allow override via environment variables
221: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_5H_THRESHOLD"); envVal != "" {
222: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val > 0 && val <= 100 {
223: 			thresholds.FiveHourThreshold = val
224: 		}
225: 	}
226: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD"); envVal != "" {
227: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val > 0 && val <= 100 {
228: 			thresholds.WeeklyThreshold = val
229: 		}
230: 	}
231: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_MIN_DELTA"); envVal != "" {
232: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val >= 0 {
233: 			thresholds.MinHeadroomDelta = val
234: 		}
235: 	}
236: 
237: 	// Check if auto-switch is explicitly disabled
238: 	if os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_DISABLED") == "1" || os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_DISABLED") == "true" {
239: 		return nil
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	result, err := account.AutoSwitchIfNeeded(thresholds)
243: 	if err != nil {
244: 		// Log warning but don't block spawn - continue with current account
245: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: auto-switch check failed: %v\n", err)
246: 
247: 		// Check if the underlying error is a TokenRefreshError and provide guidance
248: 		var tokenErr *account.TokenRefreshError
249: 		if errors.As(err, &tokenErr) {
250: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  → %s\n", tokenErr.ActionableGuidance())
251: 		}
252: 		return nil
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	if result.Switched {
256: 		// Log the switch
257: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
258: 		event := events.Event{
259: 			Type:      "account.auto_switched",
260: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
261: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
262: 				"from_account":     result.FromAccount,
263: 				"to_account":       result.ToAccount,
264: 				"reason":           result.Reason,
265: 				"from_5h_used":     result.FromCapacity.FiveHourUsed,
266: 				"from_weekly_used": result.FromCapacity.SevenDayUsed,
267: 				"to_5h_used":       result.ToCapacity.FiveHourUsed,
268: 				"to_weekly_used":   result.ToCapacity.SevenDayUsed,
269: 			},
270: 		}
271: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
272: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log account switch: %v\n", err)
273: 		}
274: 
275: 		fmt.Printf("🔄 Auto-switched account: %s\n", result.Reason)
276: 	}
277: 
278: 	return nil
279: }

281: // validateModeModelCombo checks for known invalid mode+model combinations.
282: // Returns a warning error (non-blocking) if an invalid combination is detected.
283: func validateModeModelCombo(backend string, resolvedModel model.ModelSpec) error {
284: 	// Invalid combination: opencode + opus
285: 	// Opus requires Claude Code CLI auth, opencode backend creates zombie agents
286: 	if backend == "opencode" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(resolvedModel.ModelID), "opus") {
287: 		return fmt.Errorf(`Warning: opencode backend with opus model may fail (auth blocked).
288:   Recommendation: Use --model sonnet (default) or let auto-selection use claude backend`)
289: 	}
290: 
291: 	// Note: Flash model is blocked earlier in the flow (hard error, not warning)
292: 	// Claude backend + non-opus models work but are non-optimal (using Max sub for cheap models)
293: 
294: 	return nil
295: }

297: // inferSkillFromBeadsIssue infers skill from a beads issue using labels, title, then type.
298: func inferSkillFromBeadsIssue(issue *beads.Issue) string {
299: 	// Check for skill:* labels first
300: 	for _, label := range issue.Labels {
301: 		if strings.HasPrefix(label, "skill:") {
302: 			return strings.TrimPrefix(label, "skill:")
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	// Check for title patterns (e.g., synthesis issues)
307: 	if strings.HasPrefix(issue.Title, "Synthesize ") && strings.Contains(issue.Title, " investigations") {
308: 		return "kb-reflect"
309: 	}
310: 
311: 	// Fall back to type-based inference
312: 	skill, err := InferSkillFromIssueType(issue.IssueType)
313: 	if err != nil {
314: 		return "feature-impl" // Default fallback
315: 	}
316: 	return skill
317: }

319: // inferMCPFromBeadsIssue extracts MCP server requirements from issue labels.
320: // Returns the MCP server name if found (e.g., "playwright" from "needs:playwright"),
321: // or empty string if no MCP-related label is present.
322: //
323: // This allows daemon-spawned agents to automatically get browser access when
324: // working on UI/CSS fixes that require visual verification.
325: func inferMCPFromBeadsIssue(issue *beads.Issue) string {
326: 	for _, label := range issue.Labels {
327: 		if strings.HasPrefix(label, "needs:") {
328: 			need := strings.TrimPrefix(label, "needs:")
329: 			// Map needs labels to MCP servers
330: 			switch need {
331: 			case "playwright":
332: 				return "playwright"
333: 				// Future: add more mappings as needed
334: 			}
335: 		}
336: 	}
337: 	return ""
338: }

340: // RunPreFlightChecks performs all pre-spawn validation checks.
341: // Returns usage check result for telemetry, or error if any check fails.
342: // hotspotCheckFunc is passed from cmd/orch to avoid circular dependencies.
343: func RunPreFlightChecks(input *SpawnInput, preCheckDir string, bypassTriage, bypassVerification bool, bypassReason string, maxAgents int, extractBeadsIDFunc func(string) string, hotspotCheckFunc func(string, string) (*gates.HotspotResult, error)) (*gates.UsageCheckResult, error) {
344: 	// Check for --bypass-triage flag (required for manual spawns)
345: 	// Daemon-driven spawns skip this check (issue already triaged)
346: 	if err := gates.CheckTriageBypass(input.DaemonDriven, bypassTriage, input.SkillName, input.Task); err != nil {
347: 		return nil, err
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	// Log the triage bypass for Phase 2 review (only for manual bypasses, not daemon-driven)
351: 	if !input.DaemonDriven && bypassTriage {
352: 		gates.LogTriageBypass(input.SkillName, input.Task)
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	// Check verification gate (Phase 3: Session Continuity Gate)
356: 	// Block spawn if unverified Tier 1 work exists (prevents cascade pattern)
357: 	// Independent parallel work can use --bypass-verification to override
358: 	if err := gates.CheckVerificationGate(bypassVerification, bypassReason); err != nil {
359: 		return nil, err
360: 	}
361: 
362: 	// Check concurrency limit before spawning
363: 	if err := gates.CheckConcurrency(input.ServerURL, maxAgents, extractBeadsIDFunc); err != nil {
364: 		return nil, err
365: 	}
366: 
367: 	// Proactive rate limit monitoring: warn at 80%, block at 95%
368: 	usageCheckResult, usageErr := gates.CheckRateLimit()
369: 	if usageErr != nil {
370: 		// usageErr contains formatted blocking message
371: 		return nil, usageErr
372: 	}
373: 
374: 	// STRATEGIC-FIRST ORCHESTRATION: Check for hotspots in task target area
375: 	// In hotspot areas (5+ bugs, persistent failures), strategic approach is recommended
376: 	// Warning shown but spawn proceeds (non-blocking)
377: 	if hotspotCheckFunc != nil {
378: 		gates.CheckHotspot(preCheckDir, input.Task, input.SkillName, input.DaemonDriven, hotspotCheckFunc)
379: 	}
380: 
381: 	return usageCheckResult, nil
382: }

384: // ResolveProjectDirectory determines the project directory and name.
385: // Uses workdir if provided, otherwise current working directory.
386: func ResolveProjectDirectory(workdir string) (projectDir, projectName string, err error) {
387: 	if workdir != "" {
388: 		// User specified target directory via --workdir
389: 		projectDir, err = filepath.Abs(workdir)
390: 		if err != nil {
391: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve workdir path: %w", err)
392: 		}
393: 		// Verify directory exists
394: 		if stat, err := os.Stat(projectDir); err != nil {
395: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("workdir does not exist: %s", projectDir)
396: 		} else if !stat.IsDir() {
397: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("workdir is not a directory: %s", projectDir)
398: 		}
399: 	} else {
400: 		// Default: use current working directory
401: 		projectDir, err = os.Getwd()
402: 		if err != nil {
403: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current directory: %w", err)
404: 		}
405: 	}
406: 
407: 	// Get project name from directory
408: 	projectName = filepath.Base(projectDir)
409: 	return projectDir, projectName, nil
410: }
