412: // LoadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace loads skill content and generates workspace name.
413: // The ensureScaffoldingFunc is called to check/initialize scaffolding (passed from cmd/orch).
414: func LoadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace(skillName, projectName, task, projectDir string, autoInit, noTrack bool, ensureScaffoldingFunc func(string, bool, bool) error) (
415: 	skillContent, workspaceName string,
416: 	isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool,
417: 	err error) {
418: 
419: 	// Check and optionally auto-initialize scaffolding
420: 	if ensureScaffoldingFunc != nil {
421: 		if err := ensureScaffoldingFunc(projectDir, autoInit, noTrack); err != nil {
422: 			return "", "", false, false, err
423: 		}
424: 	}
425: 
426: 	// Load skill content with dependencies (e.g., worker-base patterns)
427: 	loader := skills.DefaultLoader()
428: 
429: 	// First load raw skill content (without dependencies) to detect skill type
430: 	// This is needed because LoadSkillWithDependencies puts dependency content first,
431: 	// which means the main skill's frontmatter isn't at the start of the combined content
432: 	rawSkillContent, err := loader.LoadSkillContent(skillName)
433: 	if err == nil {
434: 		if metadata, err := skills.ParseSkillMetadata(rawSkillContent); err == nil {
435: 			isOrchestrator = metadata.SkillType == "policy" || metadata.SkillType == "orchestrator"
436: 		}
437: 	}
438: 	// Detect meta-orchestrator by skill name (not skill-type)
439: 	// This enables tiered context templates for different orchestrator levels
440: 	if skillName == "meta-orchestrator" {
441: 		isMetaOrchestrator = true
442: 	}
443: 
444: 	// Generate workspace name
445: 	// Meta-orchestrators use "meta-" prefix instead of project prefix for visual distinction
446: 	// Orchestrators use "orch" skill prefix instead of "work" for visual distinction from workers
447: 	workspaceName = spawn.GenerateWorkspaceName(projectName, skillName, task, spawn.WorkspaceNameOptions{
448: 		IsMetaOrchestrator: isMetaOrchestrator,
449: 		IsOrchestrator:     isOrchestrator,
450: 	})
451: 
452: 	// Now load full skill content with dependencies for the actual spawn
453: 	skillContent, err = loader.LoadSkillWithDependencies(skillName)
454: 	if err != nil {
455: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: could not load skill '%s': %v\n", skillName, err)
456: 		skillContent = "" // Continue without skill content
457: 	}
458: 
459: 	return skillContent, workspaceName, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator, nil
460: }

462: // SetupBeadsTracking determines beads ID and manages issue lifecycle.
463: // Returns final beads ID (empty if untracked), or error if setup fails.
464: func SetupBeadsTracking(skillName, task, projectName, beadsIssueFlag string, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool, serverURL string, noTrack bool, createBeadsFn func(string, string, string) (string, error)) (string, error) {
465: 	// Determine beads ID - either from flag, create new issue, or skip if --no-track
466: 	// Orchestrators skip beads tracking entirely - they're interactive sessions with Dylan,
467: 	// not autonomous tasks. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is richer than beads comments.
468: 	skipBeadsForOrchestrator := isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator
469: 	beadsID, err := determineBeadsID(projectName, skillName, task, beadsIssueFlag, noTrack || skipBeadsForOrchestrator, createBeadsFn)
470: 	if err != nil {
471: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to determine beads ID: %w", err)
472: 	}
473: 	if skipBeadsForOrchestrator {
474: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (orchestrator session)")
475: 	} else if noTrack {
476: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (--no-track)")
477: 	}
478: 
479: 	// Check for retry patterns on existing issues - surface to prevent blind respawning
480: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
481: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
482: 		if stats, err := verify.GetFixAttemptStats(beadsID); err == nil && stats.IsRetryPattern() {
483: 			warning := verify.FormatRetryWarning(stats)
484: 			if warning != "" {
485: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", warning)
486: 			}
487: 		}
488: 	}
489: 
490: 	// Check if issue is already being worked on (prevent duplicate spawns)
491: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
492: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
493: 		if issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID); err == nil {
494: 			if issue.Status == "closed" {
495: 				return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already closed", beadsID)
496: 			}
497: 			if issue.Status == "in_progress" {
498: 				// Check if there's a truly active agent for this issue
499: 				// OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so we must verify liveness not just existence
500: 				client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
501: 				sessions, _ := client.ListSessions("")
502: 				for _, s := range sessions {
503: 					if strings.Contains(s.Title, beadsID) {
504: 						// Session exists - but is it actually active (recently updated)?
505: 						// Use 30 minute threshold - if no activity, session is stale
506: 						if client.IsSessionActive(s.ID, 30*time.Minute) {
507: 							return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already in_progress with active agent (session %s). Use 'orch send %s' to interact or 'orch abandon %s' to restart", beadsID, s.ID, s.ID, beadsID)
508: 						}
509: 						// Session exists but is stale - log and continue (allow respawn)
510: 						fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Note: found stale session %s for issue %s (no activity in 30m)\n", s.ID[:12], beadsID)
511: 					}
512: 				}
513: 				// No active session - check if Phase: Complete was reported
514: 				// If so, orchestrator needs to run 'orch complete' before respawning
515: 				if complete, err := verify.IsPhaseComplete(beadsID); err == nil && complete {
516: 					return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s has Phase: Complete but is not closed. Run 'orch complete %s' first", beadsID, beadsID)
517: 				}
518: 				// In progress but no active agent and not Phase: Complete - warn but allow respawn
519: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: issue %s is in_progress but no active agent found. Respawning.\n", beadsID)
520: 			}
521: 		}
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	// Update beads issue status to in_progress (only if tracking a real issue)
525: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
526: 	if !noTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
527: 		if err := verify.UpdateIssueStatus(beadsID, "in_progress"); err != nil {
528: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update beads issue status: %v\n", err)
529: 			// Continue anyway
530: 		}
531: 	}
532: 
533: 	return beadsID, nil
534: }

536: // ResolveAndValidateModel resolves model aliases and validates model choice.
537: // Returns error if flash model is requested (unsupported).
538: func ResolveAndValidateModel(modelFlag string) (model.ModelSpec, error) {
539: 	// Load user config for custom model aliases
540: 	cfg, _ := userconfig.Load()
541: 	var configModels map[string]string
542: 	if cfg != nil {
543: 		configModels = cfg.Models
544: 	}
545: 
546: 	// If no model flag specified, check config default_model before hardcoded default
547: 	effectiveSpec := modelFlag
548: 	if effectiveSpec == "" && cfg != nil && cfg.DefaultModel != "" {
549: 		effectiveSpec = cfg.DefaultModel
550: 	}
551: 
552: 	// Resolve model - convert aliases to full format
553: 	// Config aliases take precedence over built-in aliases
554: 	resolvedModel := model.ResolveWithConfig(effectiveSpec, configModels)
555: 
556: 	// Validate flash model - TPM rate limits make it unusable
557: 	if resolvedModel.Provider == "google" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(resolvedModel.ModelID), "flash") {
558: 		return resolvedModel, fmt.Errorf(`
559: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
560: │  🚫 Flash model not supported                                                │
561: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
562: │  Gemini Flash has TPM (tokens per minute) rate limits that make it           │
563: │  unsuitable for agent work. Use sonnet (default) or opus instead.            │
564: │                                                                             │
565: │  Available options:                                                         │
566: │    • --model sonnet  (default, pay-per-token via OpenCode)                  │
567: │    • --model opus    (Max subscription via claude CLI)                      │
568: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
569: `)
570: 	}
571: 
572: 	return resolvedModel, nil
573: }

575: // GatherSpawnContext gathers KB context and performs gap analysis.
576: // Returns context string, gap analysis, model injection info, or error.
577: func GatherSpawnContext(skillContent, task, beadsID, projectDir, workspaceName, skillName string, skipArtifactCheck, gateOnGap, skipGapGate bool, gapThreshold int) (
578: 	kbContext string,
579: 	gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis,
580: 	hasInjectedModels bool,
581: 	primaryModelPath string,
582: 	err error) {
583: 	stalenessMeta := &spawn.StalenessEventMeta{
584: 		SpawnID:    workspaceName,
585: 		AgentSkill: skillName,
586: 	}
587: 
588: 	if skipArtifactCheck {
589: 		fmt.Println("Skipping context check (--skip-artifact-check)")
590: 		return "", nil, false, "", nil
591: 	}
592: 
593: 	// Parse skill requirements to determine what context to gather
594: 	requires := spawn.ParseSkillRequires(skillContent)
595: 
596: 	if requires != nil && requires.HasRequirements() {
597: 		// Skill-driven context gathering
598: 		fmt.Printf("Gathering context (skill requires: %s)\n", requires.String())
599: 		kbContext = spawn.GatherRequiredContext(requires, task, beadsID, projectDir, stalenessMeta)
600: 		// For skill-driven context, create a basic gap analysis from the results
601: 		// This is a placeholder - skills may provide their own gap info
602: 		gapAnalysis = spawn.AnalyzeGaps(nil, task)
603: 	} else {
604: 		// Fall back to default kb context check with full gap analysis
605: 		gapResult := runPreSpawnKBCheckFull(task, projectDir, stalenessMeta)
606: 		kbContext = gapResult.Context
607: 		gapAnalysis = gapResult.GapAnalysis
608: 
609: 		// Extract model injection info for probe vs investigation routing
610: 		if gapResult.FormatResult != nil {
611: 			hasInjectedModels = gapResult.FormatResult.HasInjectedModels
612: 			if hasInjectedModels {
613: 				// Extract primary model path from KB context result
614: 				primaryModelPath = extractPrimaryModelPath(gapResult.FormatResult)
615: 			}
616: 		}
617: 	}
618: 
619: 	// Check gap gating - may block spawn if context quality is too low
620: 	if err := checkGapGating(gapAnalysis, gateOnGap, skipGapGate, gapThreshold); err != nil {
621: 		return "", nil, false, "", err
622: 	}
623: 
624: 	// Record gap for learning loop (if gaps detected)
625: 	if gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
626: 		recordGapForLearning(gapAnalysis, skillContent, task)
627: 	}
628: 
629: 	// Log if skip-gap-gate was used (documents conscious bypass)
630: 	if skipGapGate && gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.ShouldBlockSpawn(gapThreshold) {
631: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Bypassing gap gate (--skip-gap-gate): context quality %d\n", gapAnalysis.ContextQuality)
632: 		// Log the bypass for pattern detection
633: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
634: 		event := events.Event{
635: 			Type:      "gap.gate.bypassed",
636: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
637: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
638: 				"task":            task,
639: 				"context_quality": gapAnalysis.ContextQuality,
640: 				"beads_id":        beadsID,
641: 				"skill":           skillContent,
642: 			},
643: 		}
644: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
645: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log gap bypass: %v\n", err)
646: 		}
647: 	}
648: 
649: 	return kbContext, gapAnalysis, hasInjectedModels, primaryModelPath, nil
650: }

652: // ExtractBugReproInfo extracts reproduction steps if the issue is a bug.
653: // Returns isBug flag and reproduction steps.
654: func ExtractBugReproInfo(beadsID string, noTrack bool) (isBug bool, reproSteps string) {
655: 	if noTrack || beadsID == "" {
656: 		return false, ""
657: 	}
658: 
659: 	if reproResult, err := verify.GetReproForCompletion(beadsID); err == nil && reproResult != nil {
660: 		isBug = reproResult.IsBug
661: 		reproSteps = reproResult.Repro
662: 		if isBug && reproSteps != "" {
663: 			fmt.Printf("🐛 Bug issue detected - reproduction steps included in context\n")
664: 		}
665: 	}
666: 	return isBug, reproSteps
667: }

669: // BuildUsageInfo converts rate limit check result to UsageInfo struct.
670: // Returns nil if no usage check result available.
671: func BuildUsageInfo(usageCheckResult *gates.UsageCheckResult) *spawn.UsageInfo {
672: 	if usageCheckResult == nil || usageCheckResult.CapacityInfo == nil {
673: 		return nil
674: 	}
675: 
676: 	return &spawn.UsageInfo{

678: 		SevenDayUsed: usageCheckResult.CapacityInfo.SevenDayUsed,
679: 		AccountEmail: usageCheckResult.CapacityInfo.Email,
680: 		AutoSwitched: usageCheckResult.Switched,
681: 		SwitchReason: usageCheckResult.SwitchReason,

685: // DetermineSpawnBackend determines spawn backend with auto-selection.
686: // Priority: explicit --backend flag > explicit --model > project config > user config > infrastructure detection (advisory) > default.
687: // When --backend, --model, or config is explicit, infrastructure detection becomes advisory (warning only).
688: // This prevents the escape hatch from silently overriding user intent.
689: func DetermineSpawnBackend(resolvedModel model.ModelSpec, task, beadsID, projectDir, backendFlag, spawnModel string) (string, error) {
690: 	// Load project config (used for backend default)
691: 	projCfg, _ := config.Load(projectDir)
692: 
693: 	// Load user config (~/.orch/config.yaml) for backend fallback
694: 	userCfg, _ := userconfig.Load()
695: 	_, userCfgErr := os.Stat(userconfig.ConfigPath())
696: 	userCfgExplicit := userCfgErr == nil && userCfg != nil && userCfg.Backend != ""
697: 
698: 	// Default to opencode (primary spawn path)
699: 	backend := "opencode"
700: 
701: 	// Track whether flags were explicitly set by user
702: 	// Explicit flags ALWAYS win over auto-detection (including infrastructure escape hatch)
703: 	explicitBackend := backendFlag != ""
704: 	explicitModel := spawnModel != ""
705: 
706: 	if explicitBackend {
707: 		// Explicit --backend flag: highest priority, always wins
708: 		backend = backendFlag
709: 		// Validate backend value
710: 		if backend != "claude" && backend != "opencode" {
711: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid --backend value: %s (must be 'claude' or 'opencode')", backend)
712: 		}
713: 
714: 		// Advisory: warn if infrastructure work detected but user chose different backend
715: 		if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) && backend != "claude" {
716: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Infrastructure work detected but respecting explicit --backend %s\n", backend)
717: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "   Recommendation: Use --backend claude for infrastructure work to survive server restarts.\n")
718: 		}
719: 	} else if explicitModel {
720: 		// Explicit --model flag: model choice implies backend requirements
721: 		// Don't let infrastructure detection override — the user chose a specific model
722: 		// that may require a specific backend (e.g., codex requires opencode)
723: 		// Resolution: project config > user config > hardcoded default
724: 		if projCfg != nil && projCfg.SpawnMode != "" {
725: 			backend = projCfg.SpawnMode
726: 		} else if userCfgExplicit {
727: 			backend = userCfg.Backend
728: 		}
729: 		// Advisory: warn if infrastructure work detected
730: 		if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) && backend != "claude" {
731: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Infrastructure work detected but respecting explicit --model %s (backend: %s)\n", spawnModel, backend)
732: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "   Recommendation: Use --backend claude for infrastructure work to survive server restarts.\n")
733: 		}
734: 	} else if projCfg != nil && projCfg.SpawnMode != "" {
735: 		// Config default: respect project spawn_mode setting
736: 		backend = projCfg.SpawnMode
737: 		if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) && backend != "claude" {
738: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Infrastructure work detected but respecting project spawn_mode %s\n", backend)
739: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "   Recommendation: Use --backend claude for infrastructure work to survive server restarts.\n")
740: 		}
741: 	} else if userCfgExplicit {
742: 		// User config default: respect user-level backend setting (~/.orch/config.yaml)
743: 		backend = userCfg.Backend
744: 		if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) && backend != "claude" {
745: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Infrastructure work detected but respecting user config backend %s\n", backend)
746: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "   Recommendation: Use --backend claude for infrastructure work to survive server restarts.\n")
747: 		}
748: 	} else if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) {
749: 		// No explicit flags or config: auto-apply escape hatch
750: 		// Agents working on OpenCode/orch infrastructure need claude backend + tmux
751: 		// to survive server restarts (prevent agents from killing themselves)
752: 		backend = "claude"
753: 		fmt.Println("🔧 Infrastructure work detected - auto-applying escape hatch (--backend claude --tmux)")
754: 		fmt.Println("   This ensures the agent survives OpenCode server restarts.")
755: 
756: 		// Log the infrastructure work detection for pattern analysis
757: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
758: 		event := events.Event{
759: 			Type:      "spawn.infrastructure_detected",
760: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
761: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
762: 				"task":     task,
763: 				"beads_id": beadsID,
764: 				"skill":    "", // Will be filled by caller
765: 			},
766: 		}
767: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
768: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log infrastructure detection: %v\n", err)
769: 		}
770: 	}
771: 
772: 	// Validate mode+model combination
773: 	if err := validateModeModelCombo(backend, resolvedModel); err != nil {
774: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  %v\n", err)
775: 	}
776: 
777: 	return backend, nil
778: }

827: 		ReproSteps:         ctx.ReproSteps,
828: 		IsOrchestrator:     ctx.IsOrchestrator,
829: 		IsMetaOrchestrator: ctx.IsMetaOrchestrator,
830: 		UsageInfo:          ctx.UsageInfo,
831: 		SpawnMode:          ctx.SpawnBackend,
832: 		DesignWorkspace:    "", // Will be set by caller if needed

839: // ValidateAndWriteContext validates context size, writes SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, and generates prompt.
840: // Returns minimal prompt, or error if validation fails.
841: func ValidateAndWriteContext(cfg *spawn.Config, force bool) (minimalPrompt string, err error) {
842: 	// Pre-spawn token estimation and validation
843: 	if err := spawn.ValidateContextSize(cfg); err != nil {
844: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("pre-spawn validation failed: %w", err)
845: 	}
846: 
847: 	// Warn about large contexts (but don't block)
848: 	if shouldWarn, warning := spawn.ShouldWarnAboutSize(cfg); shouldWarn {
849: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", warning)
850: 	}
851: 
852: 	// Check for existing workspace before writing context
853: 	// This prevents accidentally overwriting SESSION_HANDOFF.md from completed sessions
854: 	// Note: With unique suffixes in workspace names (since Jan 2026), collisions are rare
855: 	// but this provides an extra safety net and meaningful error messages
856: 	if err := checkWorkspaceExists(cfg.WorkspacePath(), force); err != nil {
857: 		return "", err
858: 	}
859: 
860: 	// Write SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
861: 	if err := spawn.WriteContext(cfg); err != nil {
862: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write spawn context: %w", err)
863: 	}
864: 
865: 	// Record orientation frame in beads comments at spawn time
866: 	if !cfg.NoTrack && !cfg.IsOrchestrator && !cfg.IsMetaOrchestrator && cfg.BeadsID != "" {
867: 		frame := strings.TrimSpace(cfg.OrientationFrame)
868: 		if frame == "" {
869: 			frame = strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Task)
870: 		}
871: 		if frame != "" {
872: 			if err := addBeadsComment(cfg.BeadsID, fmt.Sprintf("FRAME: %s", frame)); err != nil {
873: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to add frame comment: %v\n", err)
874: 			}
875: 		}
876: 	}
877: 
878: 	// Record spawn in session (if session is active)
879: 	if sessionStore, err := session.New(""); err == nil {
880: 		if err := sessionStore.RecordSpawn(cfg.BeadsID, cfg.SkillName, cfg.Task, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
881: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to record spawn in session: %v\n", err)
882: 		}
883: 	}
884: 
885: 	// Generate minimal prompt
886: 	minimalPrompt = spawn.MinimalPrompt(cfg)
887: 	return minimalPrompt, nil
888: }

890: // DispatchSpawn routes to the appropriate spawn mode function.
891: // Handles inline, headless, claude, and tmux modes.
892: func DispatchSpawn(input *SpawnInput, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, serverURL string) error {
893: 	// Spawn mode: inline (blocking TUI), tmux (opt-in for workers, default for orchestrators), claude (tmux), or headless (default for workers)
894: 	if input.Inline {
895: 		// Inline mode (blocking) - run in current terminal with TUI
896: 		return runSpawnInline(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
897: 	}
898: 
899: 	// Explicit --headless flag overrides all other mode decisions
900: 	if input.Headless {
901: 		return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
902: 	}
903: 
904: 	// Claude mode: Use tmux + claude CLI
905: 	if cfg.SpawnMode == "claude" {
906: 		return runSpawnClaude(serverURL, cfg, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
907: 	}
908: 
909: 	// Orchestrator-type skills default to tmux mode (visible interaction)
910: 	// Workers default to headless mode (automation-friendly)
911: 	useTmux := input.Tmux || input.Attach || cfg.IsOrchestrator
912: 	if useTmux {
913: 		// Tmux mode - visible, interruptible
914: 		// Default for orchestrator skills, opt-in for workers
915: 		return runSpawnTmux(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
916: 	}
917: 
918: 	// Default for workers: Headless mode - spawn via HTTP API (automation-friendly, no TUI overhead)
919: 	return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
920: }

924: // stripANSI removes ANSI escape codes from a string for cleaner error messages.

929: // formatSessionTitle formats the session title to include beads ID for matching.
930: // Format: "workspace-name [beads-id]" (e.g., "og-debug-orch-status-23dec [orch-go-v4mw]")
931: // This allows extractBeadsIDFromTitle to find agents in orch status.
932: func formatSessionTitle(workspaceName, beadsID string) string {
933: 	if beadsID == "" {
934: 		return workspaceName
935: 	}
936: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s [%s]", workspaceName, beadsID)

939: // registerOrchestratorSession registers an orchestrator session in the session registry.
940: // This is called after successful spawn for orchestrator-type skills.
941: // Workers do not use the session registry - they use beads for lifecycle tracking.
942: func registerOrchestratorSession(cfg *spawn.Config, sessionID, task string) {
943: 	if !cfg.IsOrchestrator && !cfg.IsMetaOrchestrator {
944: 		return // Only register orchestrator sessions
945: 	}
946: 
947: 	registry := session.NewRegistry("")
948: 	orchSession := session.OrchestratorSession{
949: 		WorkspaceName: cfg.WorkspaceName,
950: 		SessionID:     sessionID,
951: 		ProjectDir:    cfg.ProjectDir,
952: 		SpawnTime:     time.Now(),
953: 		Goal:          task,
954: 		Status:        "active",
955: 	}
956: 	if err := registry.Register(orchSession); err != nil {
957: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to register orchestrator session: %v\n", err)
958: 	}
959: }

961: // addGapAnalysisToEventData adds gap analysis information to an event data map.
962: // This enables tracking of context gaps for pattern analysis and dashboard surfacing.
963: func addGapAnalysisToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
964: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
965: 		return
966: 	}
967: 
968: 	eventData["gap_has_gaps"] = gapAnalysis.HasGaps
969: 	eventData["gap_context_quality"] = gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
970: 
971: 	if gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
972: 		eventData["gap_should_warn"] = gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps()
973: 		eventData["gap_match_total"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches
974: 		eventData["gap_match_constraints"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount
975: 		eventData["gap_match_decisions"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.DecisionCount
976: 		eventData["gap_match_investigations"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.InvestigationCount
977: 
978: 		// Capture gap types for pattern analysis
979: 		var gapTypes []string
980: 		for _, gap := range gapAnalysis.Gaps {
981: 			gapTypes = append(gapTypes, string(gap.Type))
982: 		}
983: 		if len(gapTypes) > 0 {
984: 			eventData["gap_types"] = gapTypes
985: 		}
986: 	}
987: }
988: 
989: // addUsageInfoToEventData adds usage information to an event data map.
990: // This enables tracking of rate limit patterns and account utilization at spawn time.
991: func addUsageInfoToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, usageInfo *spawn.UsageInfo) {
992: 	if usageInfo == nil {
993: 		return
994: 	}
995: 
996: 	eventData["usage_5h_used"] = usageInfo.FiveHourUsed
997: 	eventData["usage_weekly_used"] = usageInfo.SevenDayUsed
998: 	if usageInfo.AccountEmail != "" {
999: 		eventData["usage_account"] = usageInfo.AccountEmail
1000: 	}
1001: 	if usageInfo.AutoSwitched {
1002: 		eventData["usage_auto_switched"] = true
1003: 		eventData["usage_switch_reason"] = usageInfo.SwitchReason
1004: 	}
1005: }

1007: // formatContextQualitySummary formats context quality for spawn summary output.
1008: // Returns a formatted string with visual indicators for gap severity.
1009: // This is the "prominent" surfacing that makes gaps hard to ignore.
1010: func formatContextQualitySummary(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) string {
1011: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
1012: 		return "not checked"
1013: 	}
1014: 
1015: 	quality := gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
1016: 
1017: 	// Determine visual indicator and label based on quality level
1018: 	var indicator, label string
1019: 	switch {
1020: 	case quality == 0:
1021: 		indicator = "🚨"
1022: 		label = "CRITICAL - No context"
1023: 	case quality < 20:
1024: 		indicator = "⚠️"
1025: 		label = "poor"
1026: 	case quality < 40:
1027: 		indicator = "⚠️"
1028: 		label = "limited"
1029: 	case quality < 60:
1030: 		indicator = "📊"
1031: 		label = "moderate"
1032: 	case quality < 80:
1033: 		indicator = "✓"
1034: 		label = "good"
1035: 	default:
1036: 		indicator = "✓"
1037: 		label = "excellent"
1038: 	}
1039: 
1040: 	// Format the summary line
1041: 	summary := fmt.Sprintf("%s %d/100 (%s)", indicator, quality, label)
1042: 
1043: 	// Add match breakdown for transparency
1044: 	if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches > 0 {
1045: 		summary += fmt.Sprintf(" - %d matches", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches)
1046: 		if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount > 0 {
1047: 			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" (%d constraints)", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount)
1048: 		}
1049: 	}
1050: 
1051: 	return summary
1052: }
1053: 
1054: // printSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning prints the spawn summary with prominent gap warnings.
1055: // This ensures gaps are visible in the final output, not just during context gathering.
1056: func printSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
1057: 	if gapAnalysis == nil || !gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps() {
1058: 		return
1059: 	}
1060: 
1061: 	// Print a prominent warning box for critical gaps
1062: 	if gapAnalysis.HasCriticalGaps() || gapAnalysis.ContextQuality < 20 {
1063: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n")
1064: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n")
1065: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  ⚠️  GAP WARNING: Agent spawned with limited context         │\n")
1066: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n")
1067: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  Agent may compensate by guessing patterns/conventions.    │\n")
1068: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "│  Consider: kn decide / kn constrain / kb create            │\n")
1069: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n")
1070: 	}
1071: }
1072: 
1073: // runSpawnInline spawns the agent inline (blocking) using the HTTP API.
1074: // Uses CreateSession + SendMessageInDirectory to properly pass x-opencode-directory
1075: // header, ensuring the session is created in the correct project directory.
1076: // Blocks until the session completes by watching SSE events.
1077: func runSpawnInline(serverURL string, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task string) error {
1078: 	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
1079: 	sessionTitle := formatSessionTitle(cfg.WorkspaceName, beadsID)
1080: 
1081: 	// Step 1: Create session via HTTP API with correct directory
1082: 	// CreateSession passes x-opencode-directory header so the server uses the target project dir
1083: 	metadata := map[string]string{
1084: 		"beads_id":       cfg.BeadsID,
1085: 		"workspace_path": cfg.WorkspacePath(),
1086: 		"tier":           cfg.Tier,
1087: 		"spawn_mode":     "inline",
1088: 	}
1089: 
1090: 	// Calculate TTL based on session type
1091: 	// Worker sessions: 4 hours (14400 seconds)
1092: 	// Orchestrator sessions: 0 (no expiration)
1093: 	timeTTL := 4 * 60 * 60 // 4 hours in seconds
1094: 	if cfg.IsOrchestrator {
1095: 		timeTTL = 0 // Orchestrator sessions persist until manually cleaned
1096: 	}
1097: 
1098: 	session, err := client.CreateSession(sessionTitle, cfg.ProjectDir, cfg.Model, metadata, timeTTL)
1099: 	if err != nil {
1100: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create session: %w", err)
1101: 	}
1102: 
1103: 	sessionID := session.ID
1104: 
1105: 	// Step 2: Send the initial prompt with model selection and directory context
1106: 	// The directory header ensures the server resolves the correct project context
1107: 	if err := client.SendMessageInDirectory(sessionID, minimalPrompt, cfg.Model, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
1108: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send prompt: %w", err)
1109: 	}
1110: 
1111: 	fmt.Printf("Inline agent spawned (session: %s), waiting for completion...\n", sessionID)
1112: 
1113: 	// Step 3: Wait for session to complete (blocking)
1114: 	// Watches SSE events for busy→idle transition to maintain inline mode's blocking behavior
1115: 	if err := client.WaitForSessionIdle(sessionID); err != nil {
1116: 		return fmt.Errorf("error waiting for session: %w", err)
1117: 	}
1118: 
1119: 	// Write session ID to workspace file for later lookups
1120: 	if err := spawn.WriteSessionID(cfg.WorkspacePath(), sessionID); err != nil {
1121: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to write session ID: %v\n", err)
1122: 	}
1123: 
1124: 	// Register orchestrator session in registry (workers use beads instead)
1125: 	registerOrchestratorSession(cfg, sessionID, task)
1126: 
1127: 	// Log the session creation
1128: 	inlineLogger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
1129: 	inlineEventData := map[string]interface{}{
1130: 		"skill":               skillName,
1131: 		"task":                task,
1132: 		"workspace":           cfg.WorkspaceName,
1133: 		"beads_id":            beadsID,
1134: 		"spawn_mode":          "inline",
1135: 		"no_track":            cfg.NoTrack,
1136: 		"skip_artifact_check": cfg.SkipArtifactCheck,
1137: 	}
1138: 	if cfg.MCP != "" {
1139: 		inlineEventData["mcp"] = cfg.MCP
1140: 	}
1141: 	addGapAnalysisToEventData(inlineEventData, cfg.GapAnalysis)
1142: 	addUsageInfoToEventData(inlineEventData, cfg.UsageInfo)
1143: 	inlineEvent := events.Event{
1144: 		Type:      "session.spawned",
1145: 		SessionID: sessionID,
1146: 		Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
1147: 		Data:      inlineEventData,
1148: 	}
1149: 	if err := inlineLogger.Log(inlineEvent); err != nil {
1150: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log event: %v\n", err)
1151: 	}
1152: 
1153: 	// Print spawn summary with prominent gap warning if needed
1154: 	printSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning(cfg.GapAnalysis)
1155: 
1156: 	fmt.Printf("Agent completed:\n")
1157: 	fmt.Printf("  Session ID: %s\n", sessionID)
1158: 	fmt.Printf("  Workspace:  %s\n", cfg.WorkspaceName)
1159: 	fmt.Printf("  Beads ID:   %s\n", beadsID)
1160: 	// Print context quality with visual indicators
1161: 	fmt.Printf("  Context:    %s\n", formatContextQualitySummary(cfg.GapAnalysis))
1162: 
1163: 	return nil
1164: }
1165: 
1166: // runSpawnHeadless spawns the agent using CLI subprocess without a TUI.
1167: // This is useful for automation and daemon-driven spawns.
1168: // Uses opencode CLI with --format json to properly support model selection
1169: // (the HTTP API ignores the model parameter).
1170: // Includes retry logic for transient network failures.
1171: func runSpawnHeadless(serverURL string, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task string) error {
1172: 	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
1173: 
1174: 	// Build opencode command using CLI (like inline mode) to support model selection
1175: 	// The HTTP API ignores model parameter - only CLI mode honors --model flag
1176: 	// Format title with beads ID so orch status can match sessions
1177: 	sessionTitle := formatSessionTitle(cfg.WorkspaceName, beadsID)
1178: 
1179: 	// Use retry logic for transient failures (network issues, server temporarily unavailable)
1180: 	retryCfg := spawn.DefaultRetryConfig()
1181: 	result, retryResult := spawn.Retry(retryCfg, func() (*headlessSpawnResult, error) {
1182: 		return startHeadlessSession(client, serverURL, sessionTitle, minimalPrompt, cfg)
1183: 	})
1184: 
1185: 	if retryResult.LastErr != nil {
1186: 		// Wrap the error with user-friendly message and recovery guidance
1187: 		spawnErr := spawn.WrapSpawnError(retryResult.LastErr, "Headless spawn failed")
1188: 		if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
1189: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Spawn failed after %d attempts\n", retryResult.Attempts)
1190: 		}
1191: 		// Print formatted error with recovery guidance
1192: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", spawn.FormatSpawnError(spawnErr))
1193: 		return spawnErr
1194: 	}
1195: 
1196: 	if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
1197: 		fmt.Printf("Spawn succeeded after %d attempts\n", retryResult.Attempts)
1198: 	}
1199: 
1200: 	sessionID := result.SessionID
1201: 
1202: 	// Write session ID to workspace file for later lookups
1203: 	if err := spawn.WriteSessionID(cfg.WorkspacePath(), sessionID); err != nil {
1204: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to write session ID: %v\n", err)
1205: 	}
1206: 
1207: 	// Register orchestrator session in registry (workers use beads instead)
1208: 	registerOrchestratorSession(cfg, sessionID, task)
1209: 
1210: 	// Log the session creation
1211: 	logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
1212: 	eventData := map[string]interface{}{
1213: 		"skill":               skillName,
1214: 		"task":                task,
1215: 		"workspace":           cfg.WorkspaceName,
1216: 		"beads_id":            beadsID,
1217: 		"session_id":          sessionID,
1218: 		"spawn_mode":          "headless",
1219: 		"model":               cfg.Model,
1220: 		"no_track":            cfg.NoTrack,
1221: 		"skip_artifact_check": cfg.SkipArtifactCheck,
1222: 	}
1223: 	if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
1224: 		eventData["retry_attempts"] = retryResult.Attempts
1225: 	}
1226: 	if cfg.MCP != "" {
1227: 		eventData["mcp"] = cfg.MCP
1228: 	}
1229: 	addGapAnalysisToEventData(eventData, cfg.GapAnalysis)
1230: 	addUsageInfoToEventData(eventData, cfg.UsageInfo)
1231: 	event := events.Event{
1232: 		Type:      "session.spawned",
1233: 		SessionID: sessionID,
1234: 		Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
1235: 		Data:      eventData,
1236: 	}
1237: 	if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
1238: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log event: %v\n", err)
1239: 	}
1240: 
1241: 	// Print spawn summary with prominent gap warning if needed

1261: // startHeadlessSession creates an OpenCode session via HTTP API and sends the initial prompt.
1262: // Uses HTTP API instead of CLI subprocess to properly set the session's working directory
1263: // via x-opencode-directory header. This fixes cross-project spawns where --workdir differs
1264: // from the orchestrator's CWD.
1265: // Model selection is handled per-message by SendMessageInDirectory (providerID/modelID format).
1266: func startHeadlessSession(client *opencode.Client, serverURL, sessionTitle, minimalPrompt string, cfg *spawn.Config) (*headlessSpawnResult, error) {
1267: 	// Step 1: Create session via HTTP API with correct directory
1268: 	// CreateSession passes x-opencode-directory header so the server uses the target project dir
1269: 	metadata := map[string]string{
1270: 		"beads_id":       cfg.BeadsID,
1271: 		"workspace_path": cfg.WorkspacePath(),
1272: 		"tier":           cfg.Tier,
1273: 		"spawn_mode":     "headless",
1274: 	}
1275: 
1276: 	// Calculate TTL based on session type
1277: 	// Worker sessions: 4 hours (14400 seconds)
1278: 	// Orchestrator sessions: 0 (no expiration)
1279: 	timeTTL := 4 * 60 * 60 // 4 hours in seconds
1280: 	if cfg.IsOrchestrator {
1281: 		timeTTL = 0 // Orchestrator sessions persist until manually cleaned
1282: 	}
1283: 
1284: 	session, err := client.CreateSession(sessionTitle, cfg.ProjectDir, cfg.Model, metadata, timeTTL)
1285: 	if err != nil {
1286: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Failed to create session via API")
1287: 	}
1288: 	if _, err := client.GetSessionInDirectory(session.ID, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
1289: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Created session but could not verify in directory")
1290: 	}
1291: 
1292: 	// Step 2: Send the initial prompt with model selection and directory context
1293: 	// The directory header ensures the server resolves the correct project context
1294: 	if err := client.SendMessageInDirectory(session.ID, minimalPrompt, cfg.Model, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
1295: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Failed to send prompt to session")
1296: 	}
1297: 	if errMsg, err := client.WaitForSessionError(session.ID, 5*time.Second); err != nil {
1298: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Failed to monitor session for errors")
1299: 	} else if errMsg != "" {
1300: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(fmt.Errorf("%s", errMsg), "Session reported an error after prompt")
1301: 	}
1302: 
1303: 	return &headlessSpawnResult{
1304: 		SessionID: session.ID,
1305: 	}, nil
1306: }

1348:

part.text:
**Assessing session verification logic**

The current flow already verifies the session after CreateSession and before SendMessageInDirectory, plus a 5s wait for errors after sending, partially matching instructions. I need to confirm if verifying session existence after both CreateSession and SendMessageInDirectory plus a short SSE subscribe is fully implemented or requires adjustment to meet the spec exactly.

**Planning verification method and refactoring**

I'm planning to add a new verification method in the client to check session existence and briefly listen for errors after SendMessageInDirectory, then update startHeadlessSession to use it and remove the earlier redundant check. This will also shorten the SSE wait to 3 seconds and improve error handling to avoid falsely reporting success when the session fails.

209: 	// previous session. Only used for meta-orchestrator spawns.
210: 	PriorHandoffPath string
211: 
212: 	// UsageInfo contains the current account usage at spawn time.
213: 	// Used for telemetry and monitoring. May be nil if usage check failed.
214: 	UsageInfo *UsageInfo
215: 
216: 	// SpawnMode specifies the spawn backend: "opencode" or "claude"
217: 	SpawnMode string
218: 
219: 	// Design handoff fields (for ui-design-session → feature-impl handoff)
220: 	// DesignWorkspace is the workspace name from a prior ui-design-session spawn
221: 	DesignWorkspace string
222: 	// DesignMockupPath is the path to the approved mockup screenshot
223: 	DesignMockupPath string
224: 	// DesignPromptPath is the path to the prompt that generated the mockup
225: 	DesignPromptPath string
226: 	// DesignNotes are notes from the design session SYNTHESIS.md
227: 	DesignNotes string
228: }
229: 
230: // UsageInfo contains account usage data at spawn time.
231: // This is a simplified copy of account.CapacityInfo for spawn context.
232: type UsageInfo struct {
233: 	// FiveHourUsed is the 5-hour session utilization (0-100).
234: 	FiveHourUsed float64
235: 	// SevenDayUsed is the weekly utilization (0-100).
236: 	SevenDayUsed float64
237: 	// AccountEmail is the account email (if available).
238: 	AccountEmail string
239: 	// AutoSwitched indicates if account was auto-switched before spawn.
240: 	AutoSwitched bool
241: 	// SwitchReason explains why account was switched.
242: 	SwitchReason string
243: }
244: 
245: // WorkspaceNameOptions provides optional configuration for workspace name generation.
246: type WorkspaceNameOptions struct {
247: 	// IsMetaOrchestrator indicates this is a meta-orchestrator spawn.
248: 	// When true, the workspace name will use "meta-" prefix instead of project prefix.
249: 	IsMetaOrchestrator bool
250: 
251: 	// IsOrchestrator indicates this is an orchestrator-type skill spawn.
252: 	// When true, the workspace name will use "orch" as the skill prefix
253: 	// instead of "work" for visual distinction from worker workspaces.
254: 	IsOrchestrator bool
255: }
256: 
257: // GenerateWorkspaceName creates a workspace name from project, skill, and task.
258: // Format: {project-prefix}-{skill-prefix}-{task-slug}-{date}-{unique}
259: // The project prefix is derived from the project name (first 2 chars of each word,
260: // or first 2 chars if single word). Examples: "orch-go" -> "og", "price-watch" -> "pw"
261: // For meta-orchestrator spawns (via opts), the prefix is "meta-" instead.
262: // For orchestrator spawns (via opts), the skill prefix is "orch" for visual distinction.
263: // The unique suffix is a 4-character hex string to prevent collisions between sessions
264: // spawned on the same day with similar tasks.
265: func GenerateWorkspaceName(projectName, skillName, task string, opts ...WorkspaceNameOptions) string {
266: 	// Check for options
267: 	var isMetaOrchestrator, isOrchestrator bool
268: 	if len(opts) > 0 {
269: 		isMetaOrchestrator = opts[0].IsMetaOrchestrator
270: 		isOrchestrator = opts[0].IsOrchestrator
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	// Generate project prefix
274: 	var projectPrefix string
275: 	if isMetaOrchestrator {
276: 		projectPrefix = "meta"
277: 	} else {
278: 		projectPrefix = generateProjectPrefix(projectName)
279: 	}
280: 	// Skill prefix mapping (similar to Python's SKILL_PREFIXES)
281: 	prefixes := map[string]string{
282: 		"investigation":        "inv",
283: 		"feature-impl":         "feat",
284: 		"systematic-debugging": "debug",
285: 		"architect":            "arch",
286: 		"codebase-audit":       "audit",
287: 		"research":             "research",
288: 	}
289: 
290: 	// Default prefix depends on whether this is an orchestrator spawn
291: 	prefix := "work"
292: 	if isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator {
293: 		prefix = "orch" // Use "orch" for orchestrator-type skills for visual distinction
294: 	}
295: 	if p, ok := prefixes[skillName]; ok {
296: 		prefix = p
297: 	}
298: 
299: 	// Generate date suffix
300: 	date := time.Now().Format("02Jan")
301: 	date = strings.ToLower(date)
302: 
303: 	// Generate task slug from first few meaningful words
304: 	slug := generateSlug(task, 3)
305: 
306: 	// Generate unique suffix to prevent workspace name collisions
307: 	// Uses 2 random bytes (4 hex chars) for sufficient uniqueness within a day
308: 	unique := generateUniqueSuffix()
309: 
310: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s-%s-%s-%s", projectPrefix, prefix, slug, date, unique)
311: }
312: 
313: // generateUniqueSuffix creates a 4-character hex string for workspace name uniqueness.
314: // This prevents collisions when spawning multiple sessions on the same day with similar tasks.
315: func generateUniqueSuffix() string {
316: 	b := make([]byte, 2)
317: 	if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
318: 		// Fallback to timestamp-based suffix if crypto/rand fails
319: 		// This shouldn't happen in practice but ensures we never return empty
320: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%04x", time.Now().UnixNano()&0xFFFF)
321: 	}
322: 	return hex.EncodeToString(b)
323: }
324: 
325: // generateSlug extracts meaningful words from text and creates a slug.
326: func generateSlug(text string, maxWords int) string {
327: 	// Stop words to exclude - including question words, auxiliary verbs, and vague adjectives
328: 	// These rarely add meaning to workspace names
329: 	stopWords := map[string]bool{
330: 		// Articles and conjunctions
331: 		"the": true, "a": true, "an": true, "and": true, "or": true,
332: 		// Prepositions
333: 		"for": true, "to": true, "in": true, "on": true, "at": true,
334: 		"with": true, "from": true, "of": true, "by": true, "as": true,
335: 		// Be verbs
336: 		"is": true, "are": true, "was": true, "were": true, "be": true,
337: 		"been": true, "being": true,
338: 		// Demonstratives
339: 		"this": true, "that": true, "these": true, "those": true,
340: 		// Question words (rarely meaningful in workspace names)
341: 		"how": true, "what": true, "when": true, "where": true, "why": true, "which": true,
342: 		// Auxiliary/modal verbs
343: 		"should": true, "could": true, "would": true, "can": true, "will": true,
344: 		"may": true, "might": true, "must": true, "shall": true,
345: 		"do": true, "does": true, "did": true, "done": true,
346: 		"have": true, "has": true, "had": true,
347: 		// Vague adjectives/adverbs that don't add specificity
348: 		"better": true, "best": true, "good": true, "bad": true, "new": true,
349: 		"more": true, "less": true, "very": true, "really": true, "just": true,
350: 		// Common task description filler words
351: 		"need": true, "needs": true, "want": true, "wants": true,
352: 		"make": true, "makes": true, "get": true, "gets": true,
353: 		"use": true, "uses": true, "using": true,
354: 		"some": true, "any": true, "all": true, "each": true, "every": true,
355: 		// Pronouns
356: 		"it": true, "its": true, "we": true, "our": true, "they": true, "their": true,
357: 	}
358: 
359: 	// Extract words (lowercase, alphanumeric only)
360: 	matches := regexAlphanumeric.FindAllString(strings.ToLower(text), -1)
361: 
362: 	var words []string
363: 	for _, word := range matches {
364: 		if !stopWords[word] && len(word) > 1 {
365: 			words = append(words, word)
366: 			if len(words) >= maxWords {
367: 				break
368: 			}
369: 		}
370: 	}
371: 
372: 	if len(words) == 0 {
373: 		return "task"
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	return strings.Join(words, "-")
377: }
378: 
379: // generateProjectPrefix creates a short prefix from a project name.
380: // For two-part names like "orch-go" or "price-watch", takes first letter of each part.
381: // For single-word names like "myproject", takes first two characters.
382: // For three or more parts, takes first two characters of each part.
383: // Examples:
384: //   - "orch-go" -> "og" (2 parts: first letter of each)
385: //   - "price-watch" -> "pw" (2 parts: first letter of each)
386: //   - "myproject" -> "my" (1 part: first two chars)
387: //   - "my-cool-project" -> "mycopr" (3 parts: first two chars of each)
388: func generateProjectPrefix(projectName string) string {
389: 	if projectName == "" {
390: 		return "og" // Fallback for empty project name
391: 	}
392: 
393: 	// Split on hyphens and underscores
394: 	parts := strings.FieldsFunc(projectName, func(r rune) bool {
395: 		return r == '-' || r == '_'
396: 	})
397: 
398: 	if len(parts) == 0 {
399: 		return "og" // Fallback
400: 	}
401: 
402: 	var prefix strings.Builder
403: 
404: 	// For exactly 2 parts, take first letter of each (creates 2-char prefix like "og", "pw")
405: 	// This is the common case for hyphenated project names
406: 	if len(parts) == 2 {
407: 		for _, part := range parts {
408: 			if len(part) >= 1 {
409: 				prefix.WriteString(strings.ToLower(string(part[0])))
410: 			}
411: 		}
412: 	} else {
413: 		// For 1 part or 3+ parts, take first 2 characters of each part
414: 		for _, part := range parts {
415: 			if len(part) >= 2 {
416: 				prefix.WriteString(strings.ToLower(part[:2]))
417: 			} else if len(part) == 1 {
418: 				prefix.WriteString(strings.ToLower(part))
419: 			}
420: 		}
421: 	}
422: 
423: 	result := prefix.String()
424: 	if result == "" {
425: 		return "og" // Fallback
426: 	}
427: 	return result
428: }
429: 
430: // WorkspacePath returns the full path to the workspace directory.
431: func (c *Config) WorkspacePath() string {
432: 	return filepath.Join(c.ProjectDir, ".orch", "workspace", c.WorkspaceName)
433: }
434: 
435: // ContextFilePath returns the path to the context file.
436: // For meta-orchestrator skills, it points to META_ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md.
437: // For orchestrator skills, it points to ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md.
438: // For all other skills, it points to SPAWN_CONTEXT.md.
439: func (c *Config) ContextFilePath() string {
440: 	var filename string
441: 	switch {
442: 	case c.IsMetaOrchestrator:
443: 		filename = "META_ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md"
444: 	case c.IsOrchestrator:
445: 		filename = "ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md"
446: 	default:
447: 		filename = "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md"
448: 	}
449: 	return filepath.Join(c.WorkspacePath(), filename)
450: }
451: 

(End of file - total 451 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package opencode
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bufio"
5: 	"context"
6: 	"encoding/json"
7: 	"fmt"
8: 	"io"
9: 	"net/http"
10: 	"strings"
11: )
12: 
13: // SSEClient handles SSE connections to OpenCode.
14: type SSEClient struct {
15: 	URL        string
16: 	httpClient *http.Client
17: }
18: 
19: // NewSSEClient creates a new SSE client.
20: // SSE clients have no timeout since they're meant for long-running streams,
21: // but do have redirect limiting to prevent redirect loops from hanging.
22: func NewSSEClient(url string) *SSEClient {
23: 	return &SSEClient{
24: 		URL: url,
25: 		httpClient: &http.Client{
26: 			// No timeout - SSE is meant to be long-running
27: 			// But limit redirects to prevent redirect loops
28: 			CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {
29: 				if len(via) >= 10 {
30: 					return fmt.Errorf("too many redirects (max 10)")
31: 				}
32: 				return nil
33: 			},
34: 		},
35: 	}
36: }
37: 
38: // Connect establishes SSE connection and sends events to channel.
39: // This is a blocking call that reads events until the connection is closed.
40: func (c *SSEClient) Connect(events chan<- SSEEvent) error {
41: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Get(c.URL)
42: 	if err != nil {
43: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to connect: %w", err)
44: 	}
45: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
46: 
47: 	return ReadSSEStream(resp.Body, events)
48: }
49: 
50: // ConnectWithContext establishes SSE connection with context cancellation.
51: // When the context is canceled, the request is aborted and the function returns.
52: func (c *SSEClient) ConnectWithContext(ctx context.Context, events chan<- SSEEvent) error {
53: 	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", c.URL, nil)
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
56: 	}
57: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
58: 	if err != nil {
59: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to connect: %w", err)
60: 	}
61: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
62: 
63: 	return ReadSSEStream(resp.Body, events)
64: }
65: 
66: // ReadSSEStream reads SSE events from a reader and sends them to a channel.
67: // This is useful for testing with mock readers.
68: func ReadSSEStream(r io.Reader, events chan<- SSEEvent) error {
69: 	reader := bufio.NewReader(r)
70: 	var eventBuffer strings.Builder
71: 
72: 	for {
73: 		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
74: 		if err != nil {
75: 			if err == io.EOF {
76: 				return nil
77: 			}
78: 			return err
79: 		}
80: 
81: 		eventBuffer.WriteString(line)
82: 
83: 		// Empty line signals end of event
84: 		if line == "\n" && eventBuffer.Len() > 1 {
85: 			raw := eventBuffer.String()
86: 			eventType, data := ParseSSEEvent(raw)
87: 			if eventType != "" {
88: 				events <- SSEEvent{Event: eventType, Data: data}
89: 			}
90: 			eventBuffer.Reset()
91: 		}
92: 	}
93: }
94: 
95: // ParseSSEEvent parses an SSE formatted event.
96: // Returns the event type and data.
97: func ParseSSEEvent(raw string) (eventType string, data string) {
98: 	lines := strings.Split(raw, "\n")
99: 	for _, line := range lines {
100: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "event: ") {
101: 			eventType = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "event: ")
102: 		} else if strings.HasPrefix(line, "data: ") {
103: 			data = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "data: ")
104: 		}
105: 	}
106: 	// If event type not in SSE prefix, try to extract from JSON data
107: 	if eventType == "" && data != "" {
108: 		var result map[string]interface{}
109: 		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &result); err == nil {
110: 			if typ, ok := result["type"].(string); ok {
111: 				eventType = typ
112: 			}
113: 		}
114: 	}
115: 	return eventType, data
116: }
117: 
118: // ParseSessionStatus extracts status and session ID from SSE data.
119: func ParseSessionStatus(data string) (status string, sessionID string) {
120: 	// Try old format: {"status":"idle","session_id":"..."}
121: 	var old struct {
122: 		Status    string `json:"status"`
123: 		SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
124: 	}
125: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &old); err == nil && old.Status != "" && old.SessionID != "" {
126: 		return old.Status, old.SessionID
127: 	}
128: 	// Try new format: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"...","status":{"type":"..."}}}
129: 	var new struct {
130: 		Type       string `json:"type"`
131: 		Properties struct {
132: 			SessionID string `json:"sessionID"`
133: 			Status    struct {
134: 				Type string `json:"type"`
135: 			} `json:"status"`
136: 		} `json:"properties"`
137: 	}
138: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &new); err == nil && new.Type == "session.status" && new.Properties.SessionID != "" && new.Properties.Status.Type != "" {
139: 		return new.Properties.Status.Type, new.Properties.SessionID
140: 	}
141: 	// Fallback: parse as map to extract any known fields
142: 	var m map[string]interface{}
143: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &m); err == nil {
144: 		// Old format fields
145: 		if s, ok := m["status"].(string); ok && s != "" {
146: 			status = s
147: 		}
148: 		if sid, ok := m["session_id"].(string); ok && sid != "" {
149: 			sessionID = sid
150: 		}
151: 		if status != "" && sessionID != "" {
152: 			return status, sessionID
153: 		}
154: 		// New format fields
155: 		if props, ok := m["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
156: 			if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid != "" {
157: 				sessionID = sid
158: 			}
159: 			if statusObj, ok := props["status"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
160: 				if s, ok := statusObj["type"].(string); ok && s != "" {
161: 					status = s
162: 				}
163: 			}
164: 		}
165: 	}
166: 	return status, sessionID
167: }
168: 
169: // DetectCompletion checks if events indicate session completion.
170: // A session is complete when it transitions from busy to idle.
171: func DetectCompletion(events []SSEEvent) (sessionID string, completed bool) {
172: 	var lastStatus string
173: 	var lastSessionID string
174: 
175: 	for _, event := range events {
176: 		if event.Event == "session.status" {
177: 			status, sid := ParseSessionStatus(event.Data)
178: 			if sid != "" {
179: 				lastSessionID = sid
180: 			}
181: 			lastStatus = status
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	// Session is complete when it transitions to idle
186: 	if lastStatus == "idle" && lastSessionID != "" {
187: 		return lastSessionID, true
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	return lastSessionID, false
191: }
192: 
193: // ParseSessionError extracts error details from a session.error event.
194: // Returns sessionID and error message if found.
195: func ParseSessionError(data string) (sessionID string, errMsg string) {
196: 	var m map[string]interface{}
197: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &m); err != nil {
198: 		return "", ""
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	// Try new format: {"type":"session.error","properties":{"sessionID":"...","error":{"message":"..."}}}
202: 	if props, ok := m["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
203: 		if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok {
204: 			sessionID = sid
205: 		}
206: 		if errorObj, ok := props["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
207: 			if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
208: 				errMsg = msg
209: 			}
210: 		}
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	// Fallback: check top-level fields
214: 	if sessionID == "" {
215: 		if sid, ok := m["sessionID"].(string); ok {
216: 			sessionID = sid
217: 		}
218: 	}
219: 	if errMsg == "" {
220: 		if errorObj, ok := m["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
221: 			if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
222: 				errMsg = msg
223: 			}
224: 		}
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	return sessionID, errMsg
228: }

61: func TestExtractSessionID(t *testing.T) {

64: 		events  []string
65: 		wantID  string
66: 		wantErr bool
67: 	}{
68: 		{
69: 			name: "sessionID at top level (actual opencode format)",
70: 			events: []string{
71: 				`{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1766199826875,"sessionID":"ses_abc123"}`,
72: 			},
73: 			wantID:  "ses_abc123",
74: 			wantErr: false,
75: 		},
76: 		{
77: 			name: "no sessionID in output",
78: 			events: []string{
79: 				`{"type":"text","content":"hello"}`,
80: 			},
81: 			wantID:  "",
82: 			wantErr: true,
83: 		},
84: 		{
85: 			name: "sessionID in second event",
86: 			events: []string{
87: 				`{"type":"init"}`,
88: 				`{"type":"step_start","sessionID":"ses_xyz789"}`,
89: 			},
90: 			wantID:  "ses_xyz789",
91: 			wantErr: false,
92: 		},
93: 	}
94: 
95: 	for _, tt := range tests {
96: 		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
97: 			id, err := ExtractSessionID(tt.events)
98: 			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
99: 				t.Errorf("ExtractSessionID() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
100: 				return
101: 			}
102: 			if id != tt.wantID {
103: 				t.Errorf("ExtractSessionID() = %v, want %v", id, tt.wantID)
104: 			}
105: 		})
106: 	}
107: }

109: func TestExtractSessionIDFromReader(t *testing.T) {
110: 	tests := []struct {
111: 		name    string
112: 		input   string
113: 		wantID  string
114: 		wantErr bool
115: 	}{
116: 		{
117: 			name:    "sessionID in first event",
118: 			input:   `{"type":"step_start","sessionID":"ses_abc123"}` + "\n" + `{"type":"text","content":"hello"}` + "\n",
119: 			wantID:  "ses_abc123",
120: 			wantErr: false,
121: 		},
122: 		{
123: 			name:    "sessionID in second event",
124: 			input:   `{"type":"init"}` + "\n" + `{"type":"step_start","sessionID":"ses_xyz789"}` + "\n",
125: 			wantID:  "ses_xyz789",
126: 			wantErr: false,
127: 		},
128: 		{
129: 			name:    "no sessionID in output",
130: 			input:   `{"type":"text","content":"hello"}` + "\n",
131: 			wantID:  "",
132: 			wantErr: true,
133: 		},
134: 		{
135: 			name:    "empty input",
136: 			input:   "",
137: 			wantID:  "",
138: 			wantErr: true,
139: 		},
140: 		{
141: 			name:    "mixed valid and invalid lines",
142: 			input:   "invalid line\n" + `{"type":"init"}` + "\n" + `{"type":"step_start","sessionID":"ses_mixed"}` + "\n",
143: 			wantID:  "ses_mixed",
144: 			wantErr: false,
145: 		},
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	for _, tt := range tests {
149: 		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
150: 			reader := bytes.NewBufferString(tt.input)
151: 			id, err := ExtractSessionIDFromReader(reader)
152: 			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
153: 				t.Errorf("ExtractSessionIDFromReader() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
154: 				return
155: 			}
156: 			if id != tt.wantID {
157: 				t.Errorf("ExtractSessionIDFromReader() = %v, want %v", id, tt.wantID)
158: 			}
159: 		})
160: 	}
161: }

340: // TestFindRecentSession tests the FindRecentSession method.
341: // It matches by directory and creation time only (within 30 seconds).
342: func TestFindRecentSession(t *testing.T) {
343: 	projectDir := "/home/user/project1"
344: 	nowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()
345: 	// Old session (more than 30 seconds ago)
346: 	oldMs := nowMs - 60*1000
347: 	// New session (just created)
348: 	newMs := nowMs - 1000 // 1 second ago
349: 	// Other project session (even newer, but different directory)
350: 	otherMs := nowMs - 500
351: 
352: 	mockSessions := fmt.Sprintf(`[
353: 		{"id":"ses_old","directory":"/home/user/project1","time":{"created":%d}},
354: 		{"id":"ses_new","directory":"/home/user/project1","time":{"created":%d}},
355: 		{"id":"ses_other","directory":"/home/user/other","time":{"created":%d}}
356: 	]`, oldMs, newMs, otherMs)
357: 
358: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
359: 		if r.URL.Path != "/session" {
360: 			t.Errorf("Expected path /session, got %s", r.URL.Path)
361: 		}
362: 		if r.Header.Get("x-opencode-directory") != projectDir {
363: 			t.Errorf("Expected header x-opencode-directory: %s, got %s", projectDir, r.Header.Get("x-opencode-directory"))
364: 		}
365: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
366: 		w.Write([]byte(mockSessions))
367: 	}))
368: 	defer server.Close()
369: 
370: 	client := NewClient(server.URL)
371: 	sessionID, err := client.FindRecentSession(projectDir)
372: 	if err != nil {
373: 		t.Fatalf("FindRecentSession() error = %v", err)
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	if sessionID != "ses_new" {
377: 		t.Errorf("sessionID = %s, want ses_new", sessionID)
378: 	}
379: }

381: // TestFindRecentSessionWithRetry tests the retry logic for session discovery.
382: func TestFindRecentSessionWithRetry(t *testing.T) {
383: 	projectDir := "/home/user/project1"
384: 
385: 	t.Run("succeeds on first attempt", func(t *testing.T) {
386: 		callCount := 0
387: 		nowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()
388: 		server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
389: 			callCount++
390: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
391: 			// Return a session created "now" so it's within the 30s window
392: 			w.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf(`[{"id":"ses_found","directory":"%s","time":{"created":%d}}]`, projectDir, nowMs)))
393: 		}))
394: 		defer server.Close()
395: 
396: 		client := NewClient(server.URL)
397: 		sessionID, err := client.FindRecentSessionWithRetry(projectDir, 3, 10*time.Millisecond)
398: 		if err != nil {
399: 			t.Fatalf("Expected no error, got %v", err)
400: 		}
401: 		if sessionID != "ses_found" {
402: 			t.Errorf("sessionID = %s, want ses_found", sessionID)
403: 		}
404: 		if callCount != 1 {
405: 			t.Errorf("callCount = %d, want 1", callCount)
406: 		}
407: 	})
408: 
409: 	t.Run("succeeds on second attempt", func(t *testing.T) {
410: 		callCount := 0
411: 		nowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()
412: 		server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
413: 			callCount++
414: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
415: 			if callCount == 1 {
416: 				// First call returns empty
417: 				w.Write([]byte(`[]`))
418: 			} else {
419: 				// Second call returns the session
420: 				w.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf(`[{"id":"ses_found","directory":"%s","time":{"created":%d}}]`, projectDir, nowMs)))
421: 			}
422: 		}))
423: 		defer server.Close()
424: 
425: 		client := NewClient(server.URL)
426: 		sessionID, err := client.FindRecentSessionWithRetry(projectDir, 3, 10*time.Millisecond)
427: 		if err != nil {
428: 			t.Fatalf("Expected no error, got %v", err)
429: 		}
430: 		if sessionID != "ses_found" {
431: 			t.Errorf("sessionID = %s, want ses_found", sessionID)
432: 		}
433: 		if callCount != 2 {
434: 			t.Errorf("callCount = %d, want 2", callCount)
435: 		}
436: 	})
437: 
438: 	t.Run("returns error after max attempts", func(t *testing.T) {
439: 		callCount := 0
440: 		server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
441: 			callCount++
442: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
443: 			// Always return empty
444: 			w.Write([]byte(`[]`))
445: 		}))
446: 		defer server.Close()
447: 
448: 		client := NewClient(server.URL)
449: 		sessionID, err := client.FindRecentSessionWithRetry(projectDir, 3, 10*time.Millisecond)
450: 		if err == nil {
451: 			t.Error("Expected error after max attempts")
452: 		}
453: 		if sessionID != "" {
454: 			t.Errorf("sessionID = %s, want empty string", sessionID)
455: 		}
456: 		if callCount != 3 {
457: 			t.Errorf("callCount = %d, want 3", callCount)
458: 		}
459: 	})
460: }

572: // TestSendMessageWithStreaming tests the SendMessageWithStreaming method.
573: func TestSendMessageWithStreaming(t *testing.T) {
574: 	sessionID := "ses_test123"
575: 	messageReceived := false
576: 	sseRequestReceived := false
577: 
578: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
579: 		switch r.URL.Path {
580: 		case "/session/" + sessionID + "/prompt_async":
581: 			// Verify the message was received correctly
582: 			messageReceived = true
583: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
584: 
585: 		case "/event":
586: 			// SSE endpoint - send a series of events
587: 			sseRequestReceived = true
588: 			flusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)
589: 			if !ok {
590: 				t.Fatal("Expected http.Flusher")
591: 			}
592: 
593: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/event-stream")
594: 			w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
595: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
596: 
597: 			// Send session busy event
598: 			busyEvent := `event: session.status
599: data: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","status":{"type":"busy"}}}
600: 
601: `
602: 			w.Write([]byte(busyEvent))
603: 			flusher.Flush()
604: 
605: 			// Send text streaming events
606: 			textEvent1 := `event: message.part
607: data: {"type":"message.part","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","messageID":"msg_1","part":{"type":"text","text":"Hello, "}}}
608: 
609: `
610: 			w.Write([]byte(textEvent1))
611: 			flusher.Flush()
612: 
613: 			textEvent2 := `event: message.part
614: data: {"type":"message.part","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","messageID":"msg_1","part":{"type":"text","text":"world!"}}}
615: 
616: `
617: 			w.Write([]byte(textEvent2))
618: 			flusher.Flush()
619: 
620: 			// Send session idle event (completion)
621: 			idleEvent := `event: session.status
622: data: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","status":{"type":"idle"}}}
623: 
624: `
625: 			w.Write([]byte(idleEvent))
626: 			flusher.Flush()
627: 
628: 		default:
629: 			t.Errorf("Unexpected path: %s", r.URL.Path)
630: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
631: 		}
632: 	}))
633: 	defer server.Close()
634: 
635: 	client := NewClient(server.URL)
636: 
637: 	var streamedContent bytes.Buffer
638: 	err := client.SendMessageWithStreaming(sessionID, "test message", &streamedContent)
639: 	if err != nil {
640: 		t.Fatalf("SendMessageWithStreaming() error = %v", err)
641: 	}
642: 
643: 	if !messageReceived {
644: 		t.Error("Message was not sent to async endpoint")
645: 	}
646: 	if !sseRequestReceived {
647: 		t.Error("SSE request was not made")
648: 	}
649: 
650: 	// Check streamed content
651: 	content := streamedContent.String()
652: 	if !strings.Contains(content, "Hello, ") {
653: 		t.Errorf("Streamed content missing 'Hello, ', got: %s", content)
654: 	}
655: 	if !strings.Contains(content, "world!") {
656: 		t.Errorf("Streamed content missing 'world!', got: %s", content)
657: 	}
658: }

660: // TestSendMessageWithStreamingSessionError tests that session errors are properly detected.
661: func TestSendMessageWithStreamingSessionError(t *testing.T) {
662: 	sessionID := "ses_error_test"
663: 
664: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
665: 		switch r.URL.Path {
666: 		case "/session/" + sessionID + "/prompt_async":
667: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
668: 
669: 		case "/event":
670: 			flusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)
671: 			if !ok {
672: 				t.Fatal("Expected http.Flusher")
673: 			}
674: 
675: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/event-stream")
676: 			w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
677: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
678: 
679: 			// Send session busy event
680: 			busyEvent := `event: session.status
681: data: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","status":{"type":"busy"}}}
682: 
683: `
684: 			w.Write([]byte(busyEvent))
685: 			flusher.Flush()
686: 
687: 			// Send session error event
688: 			errorEvent := `event: session.error
689: data: {"type":"session.error","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","error":{"message":"No user message found in stream. This should never happen."}}}
690: 
691: `
692: 			w.Write([]byte(errorEvent))
693: 			flusher.Flush()
694: 
695: 		default:
696: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
697: 		}
698: 	}))
699: 	defer server.Close()
700: 
701: 	client := NewClient(server.URL)
702: 
703: 	var streamedContent bytes.Buffer
704: 	err := client.SendMessageWithStreaming(sessionID, "test message", &streamedContent)
705: 
706: 	// Should return an error
707: 	if err == nil {
708: 		t.Fatal("SendMessageWithStreaming() expected error for session.error event")
709: 	}
710: 
711: 	// Error should contain the session error message
712: 	if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), "No user message found") {
713: 		t.Errorf("Error should contain 'No user message found', got: %v", err)
714: 	}
715: }

842: // TestExtractRecentText tests the ExtractRecentText function.
843: func TestExtractRecentText(t *testing.T) {
844: 	messages := []Message{
845: 		{
846: 			Info: MessageInfo{ID: "msg_1", Role: "user"},
847: 			Parts: []MessagePart{
848: 				{Type: "text", Text: "Hello"},
849: 			},
850: 		},
851: 		{
852: 			Info: MessageInfo{ID: "msg_2", Role: "assistant"},
853: 			Parts: []MessagePart{
854: 				{Type: "reasoning", Text: "Thinking..."},
855: 				{Type: "text", Text: "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3"},
856: 			},
857: 		},
858: 		{
859: 			Info: MessageInfo{ID: "msg_3", Role: "user"},
860: 			Parts: []MessagePart{
861: 				{Type: "text", Text: "Follow up"},
862: 			},
863: 		},
864: 		{
865: 			Info: MessageInfo{ID: "msg_4", Role: "assistant"},
866: 			Parts: []MessagePart{
867: 				{Type: "text", Text: "Response\nWith multiple\nLines"},
868: 			},
869: 		},
870: 	}
871: 
872: 	// Request 5 lines
873: 	result := ExtractRecentText(messages, 5)
874: 	if len(result) != 5 {
875: 		t.Errorf("Expected 5 lines, got %d", len(result))
876: 	}
877: 
878: 	// Check that we got the most recent lines
879: 	expected := []string{"Follow up", "Response", "With multiple", "Lines"}

882: 	for _, line := range result {
883: 		if line == "Lines" {
884: 			foundLast = true
885: 		}
886: 	}
887: 	if !foundLast {
888: 		t.Errorf("Expected to find 'Lines' in result, got: %v", result)

890: 	_ = expected // silence unused warning

1090: // TestSendMessageWithStreamingIgnoresOtherSessions tests that we only stream from target session.
1091: func TestSendMessageWithStreamingIgnoresOtherSessions(t *testing.T) {
1092: 	sessionID := "ses_target"
1093: 	otherSessionID := "ses_other"
1094: 
1095: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1096: 		switch r.URL.Path {
1097: 		case "/session/" + sessionID + "/prompt_async":
1098: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
1099: 
1100: 		case "/event":
1101: 			flusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)
1102: 			if !ok {
1103: 				t.Fatal("Expected http.Flusher")
1104: 			}
1105: 
1106: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/event-stream")
1107: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
1108: 
1109: 			// Send busy event for target session
1110: 			busyEvent := `event: session.status
1111: data: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","status":{"type":"busy"}}}
1112: 
1113: `
1114: 			w.Write([]byte(busyEvent))
1115: 			flusher.Flush()
1116: 
1117: 			// Send text from OTHER session - should be ignored
1118: 			otherEvent := `event: message.part
1119: data: {"type":"message.part","properties":{"sessionID":"` + otherSessionID + `","messageID":"msg_other","part":{"type":"text","text":"WRONG SESSION"}}}
1120: 
1121: `
1122: 			w.Write([]byte(otherEvent))
1123: 			flusher.Flush()
1124: 
1125: 			// Send text from target session
1126: 			targetEvent := `event: message.part
1127: data: {"type":"message.part","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","messageID":"msg_1","part":{"type":"text","text":"correct"}}}
1128: 
1129: `
1130: 			w.Write([]byte(targetEvent))
1131: 			flusher.Flush()
1132: 
1133: 			// Send completion for target session
1134: 			idleEvent := `event: session.status
1135: data: {"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"` + sessionID + `","status":{"type":"idle"}}}
1136: 
1137: `
1138: 			w.Write([]byte(idleEvent))
1139: 			flusher.Flush()
1140: 
1141: 		default:
1142: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
1143: 		}
1144: 	}))
1145: 	defer server.Close()
1146: 
1147: 	client := NewClient(server.URL)
1148: 
1149: 	var streamedContent bytes.Buffer
1150: 	err := client.SendMessageWithStreaming(sessionID, "test", &streamedContent)
1151: 	if err != nil {
1152: 		t.Fatalf("SendMessageWithStreaming() error = %v", err)
1153: 	}
1154: 
1155: 	content := streamedContent.String()
1156: 	if strings.Contains(content, "WRONG SESSION") {
1157: 		t.Errorf("Streamed content should not include events from other sessions, got: %s", content)
1158: 	}
1159: 	if !strings.Contains(content, "correct") {
1160: 		t.Errorf("Streamed content missing 'correct', got: %s", content)
1161: 	}
1162: }

1164: // TestCreateSession tests the CreateSession API call.
1165: func TestCreateSession(t *testing.T) {
1166: 	title := "test-session"
1167: 	directory := "/Users/dylan/project"
1168: 	model := "anthropic/claude-opus-4"
1169: 
1170: 	var receivedRequest CreateSessionRequest
1171: 	var receivedHeaders http.Header
1172: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1173: 		if r.Method != "POST" {
1174: 			t.Errorf("Expected POST method, got %s", r.Method)
1175: 		}
1176: 		if r.URL.Path != "/session" {
1177: 			t.Errorf("Expected path /session, got %s", r.URL.Path)
1178: 		}
1179: 
1180: 		// Capture headers
1181: 		receivedHeaders = r.Header.Clone()
1182: 
1183: 		// Decode request body
1184: 		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&receivedRequest); err != nil {
1185: 			t.Fatalf("Failed to decode request: %v", err)
1186: 		}
1187: 
1188: 		// Return success
1189: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1190: 		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
1191: 		response := CreateSessionResponse{
1192: 			ID:        "ses_test123",
1193: 			Title:     title,
1194: 			Directory: directory,
1195: 		}
1196: 		json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
1197: 	}))
1198: 	defer server.Close()
1199: 
1200: 	client := NewClient(server.URL)
1201: 	timeTTL := 4 * 60 * 60 // 4 hours
1202: 	resp, err := client.CreateSession(title, directory, model, nil, timeTTL)
1203: 	if err != nil {

1527: 	err := client.WaitForSessionIdle(sessionID)

1593: // TestParseModelSpec tests the parseModelSpec helper function.
1594: func TestParseModelSpec(t *testing.T) {

1596: 		name     string
1597: 		input    string
1598: 		wantNil  bool
1599: 		provider string
1600: 		modelID  string
1601: 	}{
1602: 		{
1603: 			name:     "valid provider/modelID format",
1604: 			input:    "google/gemini-2.5-flash",
1605: 			wantNil:  false,
1606: 			provider: "google",
1607: 			modelID:  "gemini-2.5-flash",
1608: 		},
1609: 		{
1610: 			name:     "valid anthropic model",
1611: 			input:    "anthropic/claude-opus-4-5-20251101",
1612: 			wantNil:  false,
1613: 			provider: "anthropic",
1614: 			modelID:  "claude-opus-4-5-20251101",
1615: 		},
1616: 		{
1617: 			name:    "empty string",
1618: 			input:   "",
1619: 			wantNil: true,
1620: 		},
1621: 		{
1622: 			name:    "no slash",
1623: 			input:   "claude-opus-4",
1624: 			wantNil: true,
1625: 		},
1626: 		{
1627: 			name:    "empty provider",
1628: 			input:   "/modelID",
1629: 			wantNil: true,
1630: 		},
1631: 		{
1632: 			name:    "empty modelID",
1633: 			input:   "provider/",
1634: 			wantNil: true,
1635: 		},
1636: 	}
1637: 
1638: 	for _, tt := range tests {
1639: 		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1640: 			result := parseModelSpec(tt.input)
1641: 			if tt.wantNil {
1642: 				if result != nil {
1643: 					t.Errorf("parseModelSpec(%q) = %v, want nil", tt.input, result)
1644: 				}
1645: 				return
1646: 			}
1647: 			if result == nil {
1648: 				t.Fatalf("parseModelSpec(%q) = nil, want non-nil", tt.input)
1649: 			}

1687: // TestIsSessionProcessing tests the IsSessionProcessing method.
1688: func TestIsSessionProcessing(t *testing.T) {
1689: 	sessionID := "ses_test123"
1690: 	nowMs := time.Now().UnixMilli()
1691: 
1692: 	tests := []struct {
1693: 		name           string
1694: 		messages       string
1695: 		wantProcessing bool
1696: 	}{
1697: 		{
1698: 			name: "processing - assistant message with null finish",
1699: 			messages: fmt.Sprintf(`[
1700: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_1","sessionID":"%s","role":"user","time":{"created":%d}},"parts":[]},
1701: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_2","sessionID":"%s","role":"assistant","time":{"created":%d,"completed":0},"finish":""},"parts":[]}
1702: 			]`, sessionID, nowMs-1000, sessionID, nowMs),
1703: 			wantProcessing: true,
1704: 		},
1705: 		{
1706: 			name: "idle - assistant message with finish stop",
1707: 			messages: fmt.Sprintf(`[
1708: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_1","sessionID":"%s","role":"user","time":{"created":%d}},"parts":[]},
1709: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_2","sessionID":"%s","role":"assistant","time":{"created":%d,"completed":%d},"finish":"stop"},"parts":[]}
1710: 			]`, sessionID, nowMs-2000, sessionID, nowMs-1000, nowMs-500),
1711: 			wantProcessing: false,
1712: 		},
1713: 		{
1714: 			name: "idle - assistant message with finish tool-calls",
1715: 			messages: fmt.Sprintf(`[
1716: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_1","sessionID":"%s","role":"user","time":{"created":%d}},"parts":[]},
1717: 				{"info":{"id":"msg_2","sessionID":"%s","role":"assistant","time":{"created":%d,"completed":%d},"finish":"tool-calls"},"parts":[]}
1718: 			]`, sessionID, nowMs-2000, sessionID, nowMs-1000, nowMs-500),
1719: 			wantProcessing: false,
1720: 		},
1721: 		{

35: - Details are lost if not captured immediately

+## TLDR
+
+Added a post-prompt verification step for headless spawns that confirms the session exists in the target directory and surfaces session.error events. Added unit coverage for the new verification helper and ran package tests.
+

+
+## Delta (What Changed)
+
+### Files Created
+- `.orch/workspace/og-debug-fix-headless-spawn-18feb-7dc3/VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml` - Verification record for this fix

+- `pkg/opencode/client.go` - Added VerifySessionAfterPrompt helper to check session existence and session.error events
+- `pkg/opencode/client_test.go` - Added tests for VerifySessionAfterPrompt success and error handling
+- `pkg/orch/extraction.go` - Headless spawn now verifies session after prompt with a short SSE window
+
+### Commits
+- None

+

+
+- Headless spawn verification now calls VerifySessionAfterPrompt after SendMessageInDirectory, enforcing directory-scoped session lookup and SSE error detection (`pkg/orch/extraction.go`).
+- VerifySessionAfterPrompt checks GET /session/:id with x-opencode-directory and scans for session.error events (`pkg/opencode/client.go`).
+- Tests simulate session verification and session.error handling via httptest (`pkg/opencode/client_test.go`).
+

+- Verified headless spawn failures via unit-level HTTP/SSE simulation instead of end-to-end spawn to comply with worker testing constraints.

+- Worker sessions must not perform end-to-end spawn testing; use unit tests/code review instead.
